❓ infra
❌ failure4.174.17-e2e-telco5g-ptp146minfra_other HIGH
CI builder image `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` was missing, preventing ptp-operator image build and deployment.
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: CI builder image registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 was missing, preventing ptp-operator image build and deployment.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: CI builder image unavailable (manifest unknown) (affects: deployment setup / linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet wait timeout)
• Evidence:

```

[1/2] STEP 1/6: FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 AS builder

Trying to pull registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17...

Error: creating build container: initializing source docker://registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17: reading manifest rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 in registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder: manifest unknown

make: *** [Makefile:122: docker-build] Error 125

+ podman push image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 --tls-verify=false

Error: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17: image not known

```

Then operator is deployed with the unpushed image → DaemonSet never created:

```

Error from server (NotFound): daemonsets.apps "linuxptp-daemon" not found

+ '[' 1781942433 -gt 1781942431 ']'

+ return 1

```

• Root Cause: The ptp-operator Dockerfile (FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17) references a CI builder image that no longer exists at registry.ci.openshift.org (manifest unknown). The build pod's script uses set +xe / set -x (without restoring -e), so make docker-build and podman push both fail silently and the pod exits 0. The outer deploy script then runs make deploy pointing at an image that was never pushed; the ptp-operator pod cannot start, so it never reconciles the PtpOperatorConfig and the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet is never created. The 400-second wait times out and the step exits 1.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the builder image rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 was likely pruned or never promoted to registry.ci for this nightly; no source code change is responsible.
• Is it a flake? No — the builder image tag is deterministically absent; every run on 4.17 (and likely 4.18 with its equivalent tag) will reproduce this until the image is re-published or the Dockerfile is updated.
• Suggested Fix: Re-publish or pin to an available builder image in the ptp-operator Dockerfile (e.g. a quay.io mirror or a tag that is known to be retained). Additionally, restore set -e after the credential-hiding set +e block so that image build failures are not silently swallowed.
• Affected tests: e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-tests (the sole test step; no individual test names extracted because deployment never completed)


Relation Between Groups: Single root cause — the missing CI builder image cascades: build fails → image not pushed → operator pod cannot start → DaemonSet not created → timeout.


Per-Version Notes:

• 4.17: confirmed — registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 returns manifest unknown
• 4.18: same root cause expected — the equivalent tag rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18 is likely also absent; no direct evidence in this run but the failure pattern is identical

Affected Images: registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 (missing); image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 (never built/pushed)


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: HIGH — the entire ptp-operator deployment is blocked on every nightly run until the builder image is restored or the Dockerfile is fixed.

2026-07-04T06:00
❌ failure4.184.18-e2e-telco5g-ptp136minfra_other HIGH
CI builder image `registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17` was missing, preventing ptp-operator image build and deployment.
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: CI builder image registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 was missing, preventing ptp-operator image build and deployment.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: CI builder image unavailable (manifest unknown) (affects: deployment setup / linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet wait timeout)
• Evidence:

```

[1/2] STEP 1/6: FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 AS builder

Trying to pull registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17...

Error: creating build container: initializing source docker://registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17: reading manifest rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 in registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder: manifest unknown

make: *** [Makefile:122: docker-build] Error 125

+ podman push image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 --tls-verify=false

Error: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17: image not known

```

Then operator is deployed with the unpushed image → DaemonSet never created:

```

Error from server (NotFound): daemonsets.apps "linuxptp-daemon" not found

+ '[' 1781942433 -gt 1781942431 ']'

+ return 1

```

• Root Cause: The ptp-operator Dockerfile (FROM registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17) references a CI builder image that no longer exists at registry.ci.openshift.org (manifest unknown). The build pod's script uses set +xe / set -x (without restoring -e), so make docker-build and podman push both fail silently and the pod exits 0. The outer deploy script then runs make deploy pointing at an image that was never pushed; the ptp-operator pod cannot start, so it never reconciles the PtpOperatorConfig and the linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet is never created. The 400-second wait times out and the step exits 1.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the builder image rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 was likely pruned or never promoted to registry.ci for this nightly; no source code change is responsible.
• Is it a flake? No — the builder image tag is deterministically absent; every run on 4.17 (and likely 4.18 with its equivalent tag) will reproduce this until the image is re-published or the Dockerfile is updated.
• Suggested Fix: Re-publish or pin to an available builder image in the ptp-operator Dockerfile (e.g. a quay.io mirror or a tag that is known to be retained). Additionally, restore set -e after the credential-hiding set +e block so that image build failures are not silently swallowed.
• Affected tests: e2e-telco5g-ptp-telco5g-ptp-tests (the sole test step; no individual test names extracted because deployment never completed)


Relation Between Groups: Single root cause — the missing CI builder image cascades: build fails → image not pushed → operator pod cannot start → DaemonSet not created → timeout.


Per-Version Notes:

• 4.17: confirmed — registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 returns manifest unknown
• 4.18: same root cause expected — the equivalent tag rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.18 is likely also absent; no direct evidence in this run but the failure pattern is identical

Affected Images: registry.ci.openshift.org/ocp/builder:rhel-9-golang-1.22-openshift-4.17 (missing); image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/openshift-ptp/ptp-operator:4.17 (never built/pushed)


Overall Issue Class: infra_other


Overall Severity: HIGH — the entire ptp-operator deployment is blocked on every nightly run until the builder image is restored or the Dockerfile is fixed.

2026-07-06T19:00
⚠️ Test Failures
❌ failure4.194.19-e2e-telco5g-ptp197minfra_other` (dominant — control plane outage caused ~12 failures) + `test_regression` (3 failures from `bdc9209`) + `test_flake` (2 tgm hardware failures) HIGH
A mid-run control plane outage cascaded into 12 dualnicbc failures, while a main-branch test regression and a hardware flake independently caused the remaining 5.
17 failed tests
TEST MEDIUM
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).

1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.

This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details

If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: A mid-run control plane outage cascaded into 12 dualnicbc failures, while a main-branch test regression and a hardware flake independently caused the remaining 5.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~9/17 failures — Control plane progressive outage (dualnicbc): The API server became unreachable mid-run via i/o timeout, invalidating auth tokens and causing all subsequent tests to fail with Unauthorized. One additional failure (Grandmaster ptpconfig has no associated node) is a side-effect of pod eviction during the same instability window.

• 3/17 failures — Test regression: qualified profile name mismatch (dualfollower): ptp-operator commit bdc9209 (merged to main only) introduced QualifyProfileName, producing "test-slave1_test-slave1" as the log regex target. The release-4.19 daemon still emits "test-slave1" (unqualified), so the regex never matches, all three dualfollower tests time out at ~202 s.

• 3/17 failures — PTP daemon log-stream drops, environment degraded (dualnicbc Group 1): context deadline exceeded and timedout waiting for matches errors on the log-stream connection appeared simultaneously across dualnicbc and dualfollower, pointing to a cluster-wide PTP environment degradation (likely grandmaster signal or daemon availability) coincident with the early-stage control plane instability.

• 2/17 failures — T-GM clock class stuck at 248 (tgm): The WPC T-GM node never emitted CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE 7.000000 from ptp4l/ts2phc during GNSS signal-loss injection. The node appears to have never reached GNSS-locked state (class 6) before the test triggered loss, so no holdover transition materialized. Hardware-dependent, likely a transient flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Patterndualfollowerdualnicbctgm
Log-stream regex timeoutYes (3, test regression)Yes (3, infra)No
Control plane / API server failureNoYes (9)No
Clock class stuck at 248NoNoYes (2)

• The log-stream timeout error (could not find regex in log stream … context deadline exceeded / timedout waiting for matches) is the single most visible symptom across two suites, but the root causes differ: dualfollower's is a test code regression (bad regex pattern from main branch), while dualnicbc's Group 1 is an infra connectivity drop.
• The control plane outage is entirely dualnicbc-specific (12 of its failures).
• The T-GM clock class issue is entirely tgm-specific.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix the test regression immediately (recovers 3 failures, prevents regression from spreading): Revert or gate QualifyProfileName so it is only applied when the target daemon version supports qualified names. Alternatively, add a version check in GetProfileName so that against release-4.19 images the unqualified profile name is used in the regex. This is the only confirmed code bug; it will block every future dualfollower run until fixed.

2. Retry the full job to distinguish infra flakes from real failures: The 9 dualnicbc control-plane-outage failures and the 3 log-stream drops are consistent with a one-time cluster instability event on 2026-07-07. A clean retry will confirm whether those 12 failures disappear. If the log-stream drops recur without a control-plane event, investigate PTP grandmaster signal availability on the CI cluster.

3. Investigate T-GM GNSS hardware state before the holdover tests (recovers 2 failures): Add a pre-test assertion that the T-GM has achieved clock class 6 (GNSS locked) before injecting signal loss. This prevents the test from running in an already-unlocked state and makes the flake detectable rather than a silent timeout.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — control plane outage caused ~12 failures) + test_regression (3 failures from bdc9209) + test_flake (2 tgm hardware failures)

Primary classification: infra_other


Overall Severity: HIGH — 12/17 failures from infra mean the suite is not producing reliable signal; the test regression guarantees continued dualfollower failures regardless of infra health.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower tests time out waiting for a linuxptp-daemon log pattern that the deployed release-4.19 image never emits, because the main-branch test code was changed to search for a crName_profileName qualified profile name that the daemon has never logged.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Qualified profile name mismatch between test regex and daemon log output (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")

• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-8m4j8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-8m4j8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=timedout waiting for matches

```

```

Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig test-slave1 (tests 1 & 2)

Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig temp (test 3)

Timed out after 202.072s / 202.574s / 203.090s

```


• Root Cause: The ptp-operator/main test binary (compiled from TEST_BRANCH=main) calls BasicClockSyncCheck → GetProfileName → QualifyProfileName(config.Name, profile.Name), returning "test-slave1_test-slave1" (or "temp_temp" for test 3). This qualified name is passed directly to GetProfileLogID, which constructs the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-slave1_test-slave1(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\] (ptphelper.go:85, ptphelper.go:88). The linuxptp-daemon release-4.19 image, however, logs the unqualified profile name straight from the PtpProfile CRD field: output.profile_name = *nodeProfile.Name (daemon.go:1131), rendering to #profile: test-slave1 (config.go:437). The regex never matches; GetProfileLogID exhausts its 3-minute retry loop, BasicClockSyncCheck's outer Eventually times out, and all three tests fail at ~202 s.

QualifyProfileName (ptphelper.go:955):

```go

func QualifyProfileName(crName, profileName string) string {

prefix := crName + "_"

if strings.HasPrefix(profileName, prefix) { return profileName }

return prefix + profileName

}

```

GetProfileLogID (ptphelper.go:85):

```go

const logIDRegex = (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: %s(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.*)\]

renderedRegex := fmt.Sprintf(logIDRegex, ptpConfigName) // ptpConfigName = "test-slave1_test-slave1"

```

linuxptp-daemon config.go:437:

```go

configOut = fmt.Sprintf("#profile: %s\n", conf.profile_name) // conf.profile_name = "test-slave1"

```


• Breaking PR/Commit: ptp-operator commit bdc9209 ("Enforce unique profile names per node across PtpConfig CRs"), merged to main only via [PR #178](https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/178). QualifyProfileName and the updated GetProfileName are present on main but not backported to release-4.19; this job compiles its test binary from main (TEST_BRANCH=main), so the qualifying logic runs against a release-4.19 daemon that logs only the bare profile name. No companion change was made to linuxptp-daemon (neither release-4.19 nor main) to log the crName_profileName format.

• Is it a flake? No. The daemon categorically never logs test-slave1_test-slave1; every run will time out.

• Suggested Fix: Revert the GetProfileName → QualifyProfileName change in the GetProfileLogID call path on main, or add a companion change to linuxptp-daemon that prefixes the logged profile name with crName_ and ensure that companion is backported to the release-4.19 image before re-enabling this test.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (12 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The test cluster's control plane suffered a progressive outage mid-run — PTP daemons were unreachable for log streaming early on, then the API server became completely unreachable via i/o timeout, and all subsequent tests failed with Unauthorized as the cluster's auth tokens were invalidated.



Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP daemon log-stream failure during clock sync verification (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-4lg67 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-jgsb5 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

Identical pattern seen in the dualfollower suite at the same time (junit-failures.txt lines 1–26), confirming a cluster-wide environment condition, not a test-specific regression.

• Root Cause: The tests stream logs from linuxptp-daemon-container looking for a regex confirming PTP clock sync (foreign clock ID). The first failure shows the streaming connection itself was dropped (context deadline exceeded), while the others show the regex was never found within the timeout. Both the dualnicbc and dualfollower suites experienced this simultaneously, indicating the PTP environment (grandmaster signal or daemon operation) was degraded, compounded by early-stage control plane instability disrupting the log-stream connections.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no code change in release-4.19 identified; this is an environment/infra condition.
• Is it a flake? Yes — consistent with a transient infra/environment issue affecting multiple suites simultaneously.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job; if persistent, investigate whether the PTP grandmaster hardware or its upstream clock source was unavailable on 2026-07-07.

• Group 2: Grandmaster ptpconfig has no associated node (affects: "Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart")
• Evidence:

```

could not determine pod managing this ptpconfig, err: no linuxptp-daemon pod found matching ptpconfig test-grandmaster (label="ptp/test-grandmaster", nodeName="")

```

• Root Cause: The test helper (likely ptphelper.go) looks up the linuxptp-daemon pod that owns a given ptpconfig by matching the pod's node label and nodeName. The returned nodeName="" means the test-grandmaster ptpconfig has no node label selector matching any schedulable node — either the ptpconfig was never deployed on a node (setup failure) or the pod was evicted during the earlier control plane instability. This could be a side effect of Group 3's API server trouble occurring during ptpconfig deployment.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown
• Is it a flake? Yes — most likely a cascading effect of the infrastructure instability.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job; if this recurs, add a pre-test assertion that verifies test-grandmaster ptpconfig has a non-nil nodeName before proceeding with event tests.

• Group 3: API server unreachable → Unauthorized on all remaining tests (affects: "Should check whether using fifo scheduling", "Should not be able to use the uds", "Should be able to sync using a uds", "Metrics reported by PTP pods", "The slave node network interface is taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage", "Should restart ptp4l with no socket errors after SIGTERM", "Should restart phc2sys with no socket errors after SIGTERM")
• Evidence:

```

Get "https://10.8.34.117:6443/apis/apps/v1/namespaces/openshift-ptp/daemonsets/linuxptp-daemon": dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

```

```

Unauthorized

```

From the CI build log (post-test steps):

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

```

```

Error running must-gather collection: creating temp namespace: Unauthorized

```

```

error getting cluster version: Unauthorized

```

• Root Cause: 10.8.34.117:6443 is the API server VIP for the telco5g bare-metal cluster. The TCP i/o timeout (not a TLS or HTTP-level error) means the control plane VIP was completely unreachable at the network level when the "fifo scheduling" test ran its pre-check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait). After the API server recovered, all subsequent Kubernetes API calls returned 401 Unauthorized, indicating the test pod's service account token was invalidated — consistent with a control plane restart that revoked in-flight tokens. All post-test gather steps (must-gather, extra, audit-logs) also failed with Unauthorized, confirming the cluster's auth layer did not recover for this job's credentials.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — this is an infra-level cluster failure, not a code regression.
• Is it a flake? Yes — cluster control plane transient outage.
• Suggested Fix: No code change needed; investigate the telco5g bare-metal lab's control plane health on 2026-07-07 around 07:30–08:00 UTC. The cluster's API VIP (10.8.34.117) may have experienced a node failover or network partition. Retry the job once the lab is stable.


Suite Issue Class: infra_other


Suite Severity: HIGH


tgm



Suite: tgm (2 failures)


Suite TL;DR: Both TGM failures share a single root cause — the WPC T-GM node's clock_class Prometheus metric remained at 248 (Default/uninitialized) throughout the GNSS signal-loss tests instead of transitioning to 7 (PRC unlocked in-spec / holdover in-spec), indicating the GNSS holdover state-change was never signalled by ptp4l.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: T-GM clock class never transitions from 248 → 7 on GNSS loss (affects: both failing tests)
• Evidence:

```

Test: [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]

Message: Timed out waiting for clock class 7


Test: [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]

Message: expected a metric [7.000000] but got map[metric:248]

```

From cloud-event-proxy/plugins/ptp_operator/metrics/registry.go:96:

```

Help: "6 = Locked, 7 = PRC unlocked in-spec, 52/187 = PRC unlocked out-of-spec,

135 = T-BC holdover in-spec, 165 = T-BC holdover out-of-spec, 248 = Default, ..."

```

From cloud-event-proxy/plugins/ptp_operator/metrics/ptp4lParse.go:27,38,51-53 — the clock_class metric is updated only when ptp4l logs CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE ; if no such message appears, the gauge stays at 248 (Prometheus default for an unset gauge).


• Root Cause: The T-GM node never received a CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE 7.000000 log line from ptp4l/ts2phc during the GNSS signal-loss injection window. Clock class 248 is IEEE 1588's default for a GM with no external timing reference, meaning the node either (a) never achieved full GNSS lock before the test triggered loss — so there was no "in-spec holdover" period — or (b) the GNSS loss was not propagated to the ts2phc/ptp4l layer, so ptp4l never emitted the holdover clock-class change. The transition path is: GNSS-lock (class 6) → GNSS-loss → holdover in-spec (class 7) → freerun (class 248); the evidence shows the system started and ended at 248, indicating the initial locked state and the holdover transition both did not materialize during this run. Th


TL;DR: A mid-run control plane outage cascaded into 12 dualnicbc failures, while a main-branch test regression and a hardware flake independently caused the remaining 5.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~9/17 failures — Control plane progressive outage (dualnicbc): The API server became unreachable mid-run via i/o timeout, invalidating auth tokens and causing all subsequent tests to fail with Unauthorized. One additional failure (Grandmaster ptpconfig has no associated node) is a side-effect of pod eviction during the same instability window.

• 3/17 failures — Test regression: qualified profile name mismatch (dualfollower): ptp-operator commit bdc9209 (merged to main only) introduced QualifyProfileName, producing "test-slave1_test-slave1" as the log regex target. The release-4.19 daemon still emits "test-slave1" (unqualified), so the regex never matches, all three dualfollower tests time out at ~202 s.

• 3/17 failures — PTP daemon log-stream drops, environment degraded (dualnicbc Group 1): context deadline exceeded and timedout waiting for matches errors on the log-stream connection appeared simultaneously across dualnicbc and dualfollower, pointing to a cluster-wide PTP environment degradation (likely grandmaster signal or daemon availability) coincident with the early-stage control plane instability.

• 2/17 failures — T-GM clock class stuck at 248 (tgm): The WPC T-GM node never emitted CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE 7.000000 from ptp4l/ts2phc during GNSS signal-loss injection. The node appears to have never reached GNSS-locked state (class 6) before the test triggered loss, so no holdover transition materialized. Hardware-dependent, likely a transient flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Patterndualfollowerdualnicbctgm
Log-stream regex timeoutYes (3, test regression)Yes (3, infra)No
Control plane / API server failureNoYes (9)No
Clock class stuck at 248NoNoYes (2)

• The log-stream timeout error (could not find regex in log stream … context deadline exceeded / timedout waiting for matches) is the single most visible symptom across two suites, but the root causes differ: dualfollower's is a test code regression (bad regex pattern from main branch), while dualnicbc's Group 1 is an infra connectivity drop.
• The control plane outage is entirely dualnicbc-specific (12 of its failures).
• The T-GM clock class issue is entirely tgm-specific.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix the test regression immediately (recovers 3 failures, prevents regression from spreading): Revert or gate QualifyProfileName so it is only applied when the target daemon version supports qualified names. Alternatively, add a version check in GetProfileName so that against release-4.19 images the unqualified profile name is used in the regex. This is the only confirmed code bug; it will block every future dualfollower run until fixed.

2. Retry the full job to distinguish infra flakes from real failures: The 9 dualnicbc control-plane-outage failures and the 3 log-stream drops are consistent with a one-time cluster instability event on 2026-07-07. A clean retry will confirm whether those 12 failures disappear. If the log-stream drops recur without a control-plane event, investigate PTP grandmaster signal availability on the CI cluster.

3. Investigate T-GM GNSS hardware state before the holdover tests (recovers 2 failures): Add a pre-test assertion that the T-GM has achieved clock class 6 (GNSS locked) before injecting signal loss. This prevents the test from running in an already-unlocked state and makes the flake detectable rather than a silent timeout.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — control plane outage caused ~12 failures) + test_regression (3 failures from bdc9209) + test_flake (2 tgm hardware failures)

Primary classification: infra_other


Overall Severity: HIGH — 12/17 failures from infra mean the suite is not producing reliable signal; the test regression guarantees continued dualfollower failures regardless of infra health.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower tests time out waiting for a linuxptp-daemon log pattern that the deployed release-4.19 image never emits, because the main-branch test code was changed to search for a crName_profileName qualified profile name that the daemon has never logged.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Qualified profile name mismatch between test regex and daemon log output (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")

• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-8m4j8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-8m4j8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=timedout waiting for matches

```

```

Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig test-slave1 (tests 1 & 2)

Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig temp (test 3)

Timed out after 202.072s / 202.574s / 203.090s

```


• Root Cause: The ptp-operator/main test binary (compiled from TEST_BRANCH=main) calls BasicClockSyncCheck → GetProfileName → QualifyProfileName(config.Name, profile.Name), returning "test-slave1_test-slave1" (or "temp_temp" for test 3). This qualified name is passed directly to GetProfileLogID, which constructs the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-slave1_test-slave1(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\] (ptphelper.go:85, ptphelper.go:88). The linuxptp-daemon release-4.19 image, however, logs the unqualified profile name straight from the PtpProfile CRD field: output.profile_name = *nodeProfile.Name (daemon.go:1131), rendering to #profile: test-slave1 (config.go:437). The regex never matches; GetProfileLogID exhausts its 3-minute retry loop, BasicClockSyncCheck's outer Eventually times out, and all three tests fail at ~202 s.

QualifyProfileName (ptphelper.go:955):

```go

func QualifyProfileName(crName, profileName string) string {

prefix := crName + "_"

if strings.HasPrefix(profileName, prefix) { return profileName }

return prefix + profileName

}

```

GetProfileLogID (ptphelper.go:85):

```go

const logIDRegex = (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: %s(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.*)\]

renderedRegex := fmt.Sprintf(logIDRegex, ptpConfigName) // ptpConfigName = "test-slave1_test-slave1"

```

linuxptp-daemon config.go:437:

```go

configOut = fmt.Sprintf("#profile: %s\n", conf.profile_name) // conf.profile_name = "test-slave1"

```


• Breaking PR/Commit: ptp-operator commit bdc9209 ("Enforce unique profile names per node across PtpConfig CRs"), merged to main only via [PR #178](https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/178). QualifyProfileName and the updated GetProfileName are present on main but not backported to release-4.19; this job compiles its test binary from main (TEST_BRANCH=main), so the qualifying logic runs against a release-4.19 daemon that logs only the bare profile name. No companion change was made to linuxptp-daemon (neither release-4.19 nor main) to log the crName_profileName format.

• Is it a flake? No. The daemon categorically never logs test-slave1_test-slave1; every run will time out.

• Suggested Fix: Revert the GetProfileName → QualifyProfileName change in the GetProfileLogID call path on main, or add a companion change to linuxptp-daemon that prefixes the logged profile name with crName_ and ensure that companion is backported to the release-4.19 image before re-enabling this test.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (12 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The test cluster's control plane suffered a progressive outage mid-run — PTP daemons were unreachable for log streaming early on, then the API server became completely unreachable via i/o timeout, and all subsequent tests failed with Unauthorized as the cluster's auth tokens were invalidated.



Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP daemon log-stream failure during clock sync verification (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-4lg67 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-jgsb5 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches

```

Identical pattern seen in the dualfollower suite at the same time (junit-failures.txt lines 1–26), confirming a cluster-wide environment condition, not a test-specific regression.

• Root Cause: The tests stream logs from linuxptp-daemon-container looking for a regex confirming PTP clock sync (foreign clock ID). The first failure shows the streaming connection itself was dropped (context deadline exceeded), while the others show the regex was never found within the timeout. Both the dualnicbc and dualfollower suites experienced this simultaneously, indicating the PTP environment (grandmaster signal or daemon operation) was degraded, compounded by early-stage control plane instability disrupting the log-stream connections.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no code change in release-4.19 identified; this is an environment/infra condition.
• Is it a flake? Yes — consistent with a transient infra/environment issue affecting multiple suites simultaneously.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job; if persistent, investigate whether the PTP grandmaster hardware or its upstream clock source was unavailable on 2026-07-07.

• Group 2: Grandmaster ptpconfig has no associated node (affects: "Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart")
• Evidence:

```

could not determine pod managing this ptpconfig, err: no linuxptp-daemon pod found matching ptpconfig test-grandmaster (label="ptp/test-grandmaster", nodeName="")

```

• Root Cause: The test helper (likely ptphelper.go) looks up the linuxptp-daemon pod that owns a given ptpconfig by matching the pod's node label and nodeName. The returned nodeName="" means the test-grandmaster ptpconfig has no node label selector matching any schedulable node — either the ptpconfig was never deployed on a node (setup failure) or the pod was evicted during the earlier control plane instability. This could be a side effect of Group 3's API server trouble occurring during ptpconfig deployment.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown
• Is it a flake? Yes — most likely a cascading effect of the infrastructure instability.
• Suggested Fix: Retry the job; if this recurs, add a pre-test assertion that verifies test-grandmaster ptpconfig has a non-nil nodeName before proceeding with event tests.

• Group 3: API server unreachable → Unauthorized on all remaining tests (affects: "Should check whether using fifo scheduling", "Should not be able to use the uds", "Should be able to sync using a uds", "Metrics reported by PTP pods", "The slave node network interface is taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage", "Should restart ptp4l with no socket errors after SIGTERM", "Should restart phc2sys with no socket errors after SIGTERM")
• Evidence:

```

Get "https://10.8.34.117:6443/apis/apps/v1/namespaces/openshift-ptp/daemonsets/linuxptp-daemon": dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: i/o timeout

```

```

Unauthorized

```

From the CI build log (post-test steps):

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

```

```

Error running must-gather collection: creating temp namespace: Unauthorized

```

```

error getting cluster version: Unauthorized

```

• Root Cause: 10.8.34.117:6443 is the API server VIP for the telco5g bare-metal cluster. The TCP i/o timeout (not a TLS or HTTP-level error) means the control plane VIP was completely unreachable at the network level when the "fifo scheduling" test ran its pre-check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait). After the API server recovered, all subsequent Kubernetes API calls returned 401 Unauthorized, indicating the test pod's service account token was invalidated — consistent with a control plane restart that revoked in-flight tokens. All post-test gather steps (must-gather, extra, audit-logs) also failed with Unauthorized, confirming the cluster's auth layer did not recover for this job's credentials.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — this is an infra-level cluster failure, not a code regression.
• Is it a flake? Yes — cluster control plane transient outage.
• Suggested Fix: No code change needed; investigate the telco5g bare-metal lab's control plane health on 2026-07-07 around 07:30–08:00 UTC. The cluster's API VIP (10.8.34.117) may have experienced a node failover or network partition. Retry the job once the lab is stable.


Suite Issue Class: infra_other


Suite Severity: HIGH


tgm



Suite: tgm (2 failures)


Suite TL;DR: Both TGM failures share a single root cause — the WPC T-GM node's clock_class Prometheus metric remained at 248 (Default/uninitialized) throughout the GNSS signal-loss tests instead of transitioning to 7 (PRC unlocked in-spec / holdover in-spec), indicating the GNSS holdover state-change was never signalled by ptp4l.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: T-GM clock class never transitions from 248 → 7 on GNSS loss (affects: both failing tests)
• Evidence:

```

Test: [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss [Serial]

Message: Timed out waiting for clock class 7


Test: [It] [tgm-serial] PTP e2e tests WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery [Serial]

Message: expected a metric [7.000000] but got map[metric:248]

```

From cloud-event-proxy/plugins/ptp_operator/metrics/registry.go:96:

```

Help: "6 = Locked, 7 = PRC unlocked in-spec, 52/187 = PRC unlocked out-of-spec,

135 = T-BC holdover in-spec, 165 = T-BC holdover out-of-spec, 248 = Default, ..."

```

From cloud-event-proxy/plugins/ptp_operator/metrics/ptp4lParse.go:27,38,51-53 — the clock_class metric is updated only when ptp4l logs CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE ; if no such message appears, the gauge stays at 248 (Prometheus default for an unset gauge).


• Root Cause: The T-GM node never received a CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE 7.000000 log line from ptp4l/ts2phc during the GNSS signal-loss injection window. Clock class 248 is IEEE 1588's default for a GM with no external timing reference, meaning the node either (a) never achieved full GNSS lock before the test triggered loss — so there was no "in-spec holdover" period — or (b) the GNSS loss was not propagated to the ts2phc/ptp4l layer, so ptp4l never emitted the holdover clock-class change. The transition path is: GNSS-lock (class 6) → GNSS-loss → holdover in-spec (class 7) → freerun (class 248); the evidence shows the system started and ended at 248, indicating the initial locked state and the holdover transition both did not materialize during this run. Th


TL;DR: A mid-run control plane outage cascaded into 12 dualnicbc failures, while a main-branch test regression and a hardware flake independently caused the remaining 5.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~9/17 failures — Control plane progressive outage (dualnicbc): The API server became unreachable mid-run via i/o timeout, invalidating auth tokens and causing all subsequent tests to fail with Unauthorized. One additional failure (Grandmaster ptpconfig has no associated node) is a side-effect of pod eviction during the same instability window.

• 3/17 failures — Test regression: qualified profile name mismatch (dualfollower): ptp-operator commit bdc9209 (merged to main only) introduced QualifyProfileName, producing "test-slave1_test-slave1" as the log regex target. The release-4.19 daemon still emits "test-slave1" (unqualified), so the regex never matches, all three dualfollower tests time out at ~202 s.

• 3/17 failures — PTP daemon log-stream drops, environment degraded (dualnicbc Group 1): context deadline exceeded and timedout waiting for matches errors on the log-stream connection appeared simultaneously across dualnicbc and dualfollower, pointing to a cluster-wide PTP environment degradation (likely grandmaster signal or daemon availability) coincident with the early-stage control plane instability.

• 2/17 failures — T-GM clock class stuck at 248 (tgm): The WPC T-GM node never emitted CLOCK_CLASS_CHANGE 7.000000 from ptp4l/ts2phc during GNSS signal-loss injection. The node appears to have never reached GNSS-locked state (class 6) before the test triggered loss, so no holdover transition materialized. Hardware-dependent, likely a transient flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Patterndualfollowerdualnicbctgm
Log-stream regex timeoutYes (3, test regression)Yes (3, infra)No
Control plane / API server failureNoYes (9)No
Clock class stuck at 248NoNoYes (2)

• The log-stream timeout error (could not find regex in log stream … context deadline exceeded / timedout waiting for matches) is the single most visible symptom across two suites, but the root causes differ: dualfollower's is a test code regression (bad regex pattern from main branch), while dualnicbc's Group 1 is an infra connectivity drop.
• The control plane outage is entirely dualnicbc-specific (12 of its failures).
• The T-GM clock class issue is entirely tgm-specific.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix the test regression immediately (recovers 3 failures, prevents regression from spreading): Revert or gate QualifyProfileName so it is only applied when the target daemon version supports qualified names. Alternatively, add a version check in GetProfileName so that against release-4.19 images the unqualified profile name is used in the regex. This is the only confirmed code bug; it will block every future dualfollower run until fixed.

2. Retry the full job to distinguish infra flakes from real failures: The 9 dualnicbc control-plane-outage failures and the 3 log-stream drops are consistent with a one-time cluster instability event on 2026-07-07. A clean retry will confirm whether those 12 failures disappear. If the log-stream drops recur without a control-plane event, investigate PTP grandmaster signal availability on the CI cluster.

3. Investigate T-GM GNSS hardware state before the holdover tests (recovers 2 failures): Add a pre-test assertion that the T-GM has achieved clock class 6 (GNSS locked) before injecting signal loss. This prevents the test from running in an already-unlocked state and makes the flake detectable rather than a silent timeout.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — control plane outage caused ~12 failures) + test_regression (3 failures from bdc9209) + test_flake (2 tgm hardware failures)

Primary classification: infra_other


Overall Severity: HIGH — 12/17 failures from infra mean the suite is not producing reliable signal; the test regression guarantees continued dualfollower failures regardless of infra health.




Per-Suite Details

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-8m4j8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

No companion change was made to linuxptp-daemon (neither release-4.19 nor main) to log the crName_profileName format.

• Suggested Fix: Revert the GetProfileName → QualifyProfileName change in the GetProfileLogID call path on main, or add a companion change to linuxptp-daemon that prefixes the logged profile name with crName_ and ensure that companion is backported to the release-4.19 image before re-enabling this test.
