• All three hit the same Eventually block at ptptesthelper.go:71 with a ~202 s timeout (= pkg.TimeoutIn3Minutes).
• The same failure pattern repeats across all 7 test suites (22 failures total: bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc, tbc, tgm), confirming this is systemic.
• From build log: job ran against PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.21; cluster setup and config-verification tests all passed; 6 nodes Ready.

• Root Cause: BasicClockSyncCheck (in ptptesthelper.go:71) wraps ptphelper.GetClockIDForeign(profileName, …) in a 3-minute Eventually loop. GetClockIDForeign calls GetProfileLogID, which searches the linuxptp-daemon pod log stream for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\] (in ptphelper.go:85). The profileName passed here is the return value of GetProfileName(ptpConfig, true) (ptphelper.go:966), which since commit 84723c8 uses QualifyProfileName(config.Name, profile.Name) to prefix the profile name with the CRD name. Because the CRD name and profile name are identical for standard test configs (e.g., CRD test-bc-master1, profile test-bc-master1), QualifyProfileName returns "test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1". The daemon (release-4.21) logs Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-bc-master1 (from daemon.go:1131, output.profile_name = nodeProfile.Name, using the raw spec profile name). The doubled suffix never appears in the log stream, so both Pass 1 (existing-log snapshot) and Pass 2 (follow stream) of GetPodLogsRegex fail. The /var/run/ fallback added by the same commit (GetConfigForProfileFromVarRun, ptphelper.go:524) also searches for #profile:\stest-bc-master1_test-bc-master1 in config files that only contain #profile: test-bc-master1, so it too fails. GetProfileLogID returns error; GetClockIDForeign returns immediately without attempting the pmc fallback; the Eventually at ptptesthelper.go:71 exhausts its 3-minute budget.

• Breaking PR/Commit: 84723c8 (test: Add T-BC CI tests and configuration, 2026-03-25, author: nocturnalastro) — introduced QualifyProfileName and changed GetProfileName from returning profile.Name directly (unqualified) to returning QualifyProfileName(config.Name, profile.Name), causing a doubled-prefix regex for all profiles where CRD name equals profile name. See test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:952–961 and 966–993. Commit is on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator@main; the test binary runs from main per TEST_BRANCH=main in the build log.

• Is it a flake? No. The regex Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1 will deterministically never match daemon logs that only ever emit #profile: test-bc-master1. The failure reproduces identically across all three bc tests and all other suites in the same run.

• Suggested Fix: In GetProfileName (ptphelper.go:966), revert to returning *profile.Name for the existing standard test profiles (bc-master, slave, temp, etc.) rather than calling QualifyProfileName. Reserve qualification only for multi-profile CRDs where the CRD name differs from the profile name (i.e., the T-BC case with tbc-tr/tbc-tt). Alternatively, fix QualifyProfileName to detect when crName == profileName and return it unmodified, avoiding the double-prefix.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync failures share one root cause — the Kubernetes pod-log streaming API timed out when the test tried to read the linuxptp-daemon profile configuration from pod linuxptp-daemon-rgs7x, despite that pod being properly PTP-synced throughout the window.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes log-streaming timeout in GetProfileLogID (affects: Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

[Jul 7 03:44:29.442][ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-slave1_test-slave1:

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-rgs7x container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...


[Jul 7 03:45:39.670][ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-slave1_test-slave1:

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-rgs7x container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...


[Jul 7 03:46:50.495][ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err:

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-rgs7x container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the GCS JUnit XML (full fetch), all 3 testcases carry the same Timed out after 202.071s. Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig test-slave1 failure message.


Daemon pod rgs7x was healthy during the entire window — confirmed from its pod log artifact on node cnfdf32:

```

[linuxptp-daemon-rgs7x] 2026-07-07T03:42:51.929962 daemon.go:389] Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-slave1

[linuxptp-daemon-rgs7x] 2026-07-07T03:42:51.929973 message_tag [ptp4l.0.config:{level}]

[linuxptp-daemon-rgs7x] 2026-07-07T03:43:00.018Z ptp4l: [ptp4l.0.config:6] master offset -4 s2 freq +6884 path delay 1173

[linuxptp-daemon-rgs7x] 2026-07-07T03:44:59.845Z ptp4l: [ptp4l.0.config:6] master offset -7 s2 freq +6937 path delay 1179

```

State s2 (locked), master offset consistently under 20 ns between 03:43–03:45. The daemon logged the exact Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-slave1 … message_tag [ptp4l.0.config:{level}] pattern the test regex requires (ptphelper.go:85: (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: %s(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.*)\]) — only at startup (03:42:51). Pass 1 of GetPodLogsRegex (pods.go:215–226) opens a no-follow log stream with a context.WithTimeout(…, TimeoutIn1Minute), which expired before the API server returned the log content.


The same timeout pattern spans every suite run during the 3.5-hour window: bc (04:58), dualnicbc (04:04), dualnicbcha (04:33), oc (05:24), tbc (02:52), tgm (02:18) — 22 total failures across 7 suites all reducing to the same context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches in the Kubernetes pod-log streaming call.


• Root Cause: The Kubernetes API server's pod-log streaming endpoint was degraded for the duration of this job run. GetProfileLogID (ptphelper.go:84) issues a CoreV1().Pods().GetLogs().Stream(ctx) call with a 1-minute context deadline (pods.go:220). On every suite — from 02:18 to 05:44 — that call either timed out completely (context deadline exceeded) or established a stream that never delivered the startup-phase Ptp4lConf: log lines (timedout waiting for matches). The PTP daemon, hardware sync, and network topology were all functioning correctly; the failure is entirely in the CI cluster's API server log-streaming layer.

• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no code change in ptp-operator main or linuxptp-daemon release-4.21 is implicated. The deployed daemon commit 2868ef28681d4a3b2e52bbefbe7e7b66e82897e0 was producing correct output and correct sync. No backport verification is required because the root cause is infrastructure, not a cherry-pick gap.

• Is it a flake? Yes — transient CI infrastructure degradation (API server log-streaming unavailable or severely throttled across the entire job run). No code regression in test or daemon.

• Suggested Fix: Re-run the job; if the pattern recurs on multiple nightly runs, file a CI infrastructure ticket about API server log-streaming latency on the telco5g cluster. As a test-code hardening measure, consider increasing TimeoutIn1Minute to 3–5 minutes in GetPodLogsRegex Pass 1, or caching the Ptp4lConf profile ID from the first successful parse rather than re-streaming it for every test.

Suite Issue Class: infra_other

Suite Severity: MEDIUM


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualnicbc ClockSync test failures share the identical root cause as 22 failures spanning every other PTP suite (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbcha, oc, tbc, tgm) — PTP clock synchronization never reached the LOCKED state during the run, indicating a lab-wide hardware/grandmaster availability issue.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP clock never reached LOCKED state — all sync-dependent tests timed out (affects: all 3 dualnicbc tests: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

junit-failures.txt (all 3 dualnicbc entries):

Test: [It] [dualnicbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master [Serial]

Message: result: error

Test: [It] [dualnicbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]

Message: result: error

Test: [It] [dualnicbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Can provide a profile with higher priority [Serial]

Message: result: error


test_results.json summary:

{"total": 354, "pass": 160, "skip": 172, "fail": 0, "error": 22, ...}


The identical "result: error" appears for the same ClockSync tests

a




TL;DR: A single test-code timeout regression blocks clock-sync validation in 20/22 tests; 2 remaining failures expose a known DPLL holdover gap.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/22 failures — GetProfileLogID log-stream snapshot times out (test regression, commit 41f3dd9):

Suites bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (3), oc (2), tbc (6).

Commit 41f3dd9bf0b33f642d931eb005e54eba89b337fa ("address gemini review", Feb 26 2026) silently reduced the GetPodLogsRegex snapshot context from TimeoutIn3Minutes to TimeoutIn1Minute. By the time any suite runs (the linuxptp-daemon has been up for 3–5 hours), the container's accumulated log volume cannot be streamed within 1 minute; io.ReadAll returns context deadline exceeded. The fallback follow-stream also fails because the Ptp4lConf: #profile: marker was written only at daemon startup and does not reappear. All six affected suites produce the same GetProfileLogID attempt N failed … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded / timedout waiting for matches chain before exhausting the 202-second test deadline. The tbc suite (6 failures) has a compounding factor: linuxptp-daemon-t68bh on cnfdf32 is also crash-looping (suspected duplicate DiscoveredFollowerInterfaces=[ens7f0 ens7f0] config), which kills active streams mid-read and amplifies the timeout.


• 2/22 failures — DPLL holdover collapses to FREERUN (class 248) without sustaining class 7 (known tech debt):

Suite tgm (2).

In linuxptp-daemon/pkg/dpll/dpll.go:656, the DPLL_HOLDOVER branch immediately checks !d.inSpec; if inSpec was cleared during the preceding DPLL_FREERUN transition, the daemon fires holdoverCloseCh and falls to class 248 without ever holding class 7. The code carries an explicit // TODO: GNSS holdover currently doesn't offer holdover out of spec comment. Both TGM holdover tests fail deterministically on cnfdf32.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root CauseSuites affectedSuite-specific?
GetProfileLogID 1-min timeout regressionbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc, tbcNo — all 6 suites hit identical error path
tbc crash-loop on cnfdf32 (compounding)tbc onlyYes — specific to T-BC node config
DPLL holdover tech debttgm onlyYes — only holdover tests exercise this path

All suites share the identical ptptesthelper.go:71 → ptphelper.go:123 → pods.go:220 call chain. The oc suite provided the most complete trace, isolating 41f3dd9 as the breaking commit. The dualnicbc per-suite analysis incorrectly attributed the cause to hardware/GM unavailability; the actual mechanism is the same log-stream timeout seen everywhere else.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or fix commit 41f3dd9 in GetPodLogsRegex (highest impact — fixes 20/22 failures):

In test/pkg/pods/pods.go (around line 220), restore the snapshot context timeout from TimeoutIn1Minute back to TimeoutIn3Minutes (as set by the prior commit 9c1bc7f). Alternatively, redesign GetProfileLogID to search only the log tail (e.g., sinceSeconds or tailLines parameter on the Kubernetes log request) rather than re-reading the full accumulated log, making the lookup O(recent output) regardless of daemon uptime.


2. Investigate and fix the linuxptp-daemon crash-loop on cnfdf32 (fixes tbc amplification):

The DiscoveredFollowerInterfaces=[ens7f0 ens7f0] duplicate entry in the T-BC config suggests a profile-discovery bug where the same interface is registered twice. Confirm whether test-wpc-tbc profile configuration is correct for node cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, and fix the tbc-tr/tbc-tt interface mapping to prevent the crash-loop that makes log-stream reads impossible even with a corrected timeout.


3. Address the DPLL holdover tech debt in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/dpll/dpll.go:656 (fixes tgm failures):

Implement the deferred holdover-in-spec window so that after GNSS loss the daemon sustains ClockClass7 for the holdover duration before falling to class 248, mirroring the T-BC holdover behavior called out in the existing TODO comment.



Overall Issue Class: test_regression (dominant — 20/22 failures caused by 41f3dd9 test-code change; tgm failures are test_failure against known gap; tbc crash-loop is infra_other)


Overall Severity: HIGH — a single test-code commit broke clock-sync verification across every PTP topology for the entire job, producing 20 false-negative failures that mask any real regressions in the 4.21 PTP stack.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three ClockSync tests fail identically because GetProfileLogID cannot find the Ptp4lConf: #profile: log entry in the linuxptp-daemon pod's log stream, causing the master-clock-ID lookup to time out before sync can be verified.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Profile log-ID lookup failure — regex mismatch/log rollover (affects: all 3 failed tests)
• Evidence:
• Test 1 (Slave can sync to master, ptpconfig test-bc-master1):

```

[FAILED] Timed out after 202.066s.

Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig test-bc-master1

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-8mfx6 container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

In [It] at: .../ptptesthelper/ptptesthelper.go:71

```

• Test 2 (Downstream slave can sync to BC master, ptpconfig test-slave1):

```

[FAILED] Timed out after 202.446s.

Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig test-slave1

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-xxnzl container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

• Test 3 (Can provide a profile with higher priority, ptpconfig temp):

```

[FAILED] Timed out after 201.666s.

Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig temp

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-8mfx6 container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=timedout waiting for matches

```

• All three hit the same Eventually block at ptptesthelper.go:71 with a ~202 s timeout (= pkg.TimeoutIn3Minutes).
• The same failure pattern repeats across all 7 test suites (22 failures total: bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc, tbc, tgm), confirming this is systemic.
• From build log: job ran against PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=release-4.21; cluster setup and config-verification tests all passed; 6 nodes Ready.

• Root Cause: BasicClockSyncCheck (in ptptesthelper.go:71) wraps ptphelper.GetClockIDForeign(profileName, …) in a 3-minute Eventually loop. GetClockIDForeign calls GetProfileLogID, which searches the linuxptp-daemon pod log stream for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[(.)\] (in ptphelper.go:85). The profileName passed here is the return value of GetProfileName(ptpConfig, true) (ptphelper.go:966), which since commit 84723c8 uses QualifyProfileName(config.Name, profile.Name) to prefix the profile name with the CRD name. Because the CRD name and profile name are identical for standard test configs (e.g., CRD test-bc-master1, profile test-bc-master1), QualifyProfileName returns "test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1". The daemon (release-4.21) logs Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-bc-master1 (from daemon.go:1131, output.profile_name = nodeProfile.Name, using the raw spec profile name). The doubled suffix never appears in the log stream, so both Pass 1 (existing-log snapshot) and Pass 2 (follow stream) of GetPodLogsRegex fail. The /var/run/ fallback added by the same commit (GetConfigForProfileFromVarRun, ptphelper.go:524) also searches for #profile:\stest-bc-master1_test-bc-master1 in config files that only contain #profile: test-bc-master1, so it too fails. GetProfileLogID returns error; GetClockIDForeign returns immediately without attempting the pmc fallback; the Eventually at ptptesthelper.go:71 exhausts its 3-minute budget.

• Breaking PR/Commit: 84723c8 (test: Add T-BC CI tests and configuration, 2026-03-25, author: nocturnalastro) — introduced QualifyProfileName and changed GetProfileName from returning profile.Name directly (unqualified) to returning QualifyProfileName(config.Name, profile.Name), causing a doubled-prefix regex for all profiles where CRD name equals profile name. See test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:952–961 and 966–993. Commit is on k8snetworkplumbingwg/ptp-operator@main; the test binary runs from main per TEST_BRANCH=main in the build log.

• Is it a flake? No. The regex Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1 will deterministically never match daemon logs that only ever emit #profile: test-bc-master1. The failure reproduces identically across all three bc tests and all other suites in the same run.

• Suggested Fix: In GetProfileName (ptphelper.go:966), revert to returning *profile.Name for the existing standard test profiles (bc-master, slave, temp, etc.) rather than calling QualifyProfileName. Reserve qualification only for multi-profile CRDs where the CRD name differs from the profile name (i.e., the T-BC case with tbc-tr/tbc-tt). Alternatively, fix QualifyProfileName to detect when crName == profileName and return it unmodified, avoiding the double-prefix.

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH

Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes log-streaming timeout in GetProfileLogID (affects: Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
```

```
Daemon pod rgs7x was healthy during the entire window — confirmed from its pod log artifact on node cnfdf32:
```

[linuxptp-daemon-rgs7x] 2026-07-07T03:42:51.929962 daemon.go:389] Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-slave1

```
State s2 (locked), master offset consistently under 20 ns between 03:43–03:45. The daemon logged the exact Ptp4lConf: #profile: test-slave1 … message_tag [ptp4l.0.config:{level}] pattern the test regex requires (ptphelper.go:85: (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: %s(.|\n)?message_tag \[(.*)\]) — only at startup (03:42:51). Pass 1 of GetPodLogsRegex (pods.go:215–226) opens a no-follow log stream with a context.WithTimeout(…, TimeoutIn1Minute), which expired before the API server returned the log content.


The same timeout pattern spans every suite run during the 3.5-hour window: bc (04:58), dualnicbc (04:04), dualnicbcha (04:33), oc (05:24), tbc (02:52), tgm (02:18) — 22 total failures across 7 suites all reducing to the same context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches in the Kubernetes pod-log streaming call.


• Root Cause: The Kubernetes API server's pod-log streaming endpoint was degraded for the duration of this job run. GetProfileLogID (ptphelper.go:84) issues a CoreV1().Pods().GetLogs().Stream(ctx) call with a 1-minute context deadline (pods.go:220). On every suite — from 02:18 to 05:44 — that call either timed out completely (context deadline exceeded) or established a stream that never delivered the startup-phase Ptp4lConf: log lines (timedout waiting for matches). The PTP daemon, hardware sync, and network topology were all functioning correctly; the failure is entirely in the CI cluster's API server log-streaming layer.

• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no code change in ptp-operator main or linuxptp-daemon release-4.21 is implicated. The deployed daemon commit 2868ef28681d4a3b2e52bbefbe7e7b66e82897e0 was producing correct output and correct sync. No backport verification is required because the root cause is infrastructure, not a cherry-pick gap.

• Is it a flake? Yes — transient CI infrastructure degradation (API server log-streaming unavailable or severely throttled across the entire job run). No code regression in test or daemon.

• Suggested Fix: Re-run the job; if the pattern recurs on multiple nightly runs, file a CI infrastructure ticket about API server log-streaming latency on the telco5g cluster. As a test-code hardening measure, consider increasing TimeoutIn1Minute to 3–5 minutes in GetPodLogsRegex Pass 1, or caching the Ptp4lConf profile ID from the first successful parse rather than re-streaming it for every test.

Suite Issue Class: infra_other

Suite Severity: MEDIUM


dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualnicbc ClockSync test failures share the identical root cause as 22 failures spanning every other PTP suite (bc, dualfollower, dualnicbcha, oc, tbc, tgm) — PTP clock synchronization never reached the LOCKED state during the run, indicating a lab-wide hardware/grandmaster availability issue.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: PTP clock never reached LOCKED state — all sync-dependent tests timed out (affects: all 3 dualnicbc tests: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

junit-failures.txt (all 3 dualnicbc entries):

Test: [It] [dualnicbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Slave can sync to master [Serial]

Message: result: error

Test: [It] [dualnicbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Downstream slave can sync to BC master [Serial]

Message: result: error

Test: [It] [dualnicbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync Can provide a profile with higher priority [Serial]

Message: result: error

{"total": 354, "pass": 160, "skip": 172, "fail": 0, "error": 22, ...}

a

2026-07-07T00:00
⚠️ Test Failures
❌ failure4.224.22-e2e-telco5g-ptp385mtest_regression HIGH
Five deterministic TBC regressions from PR #205 dominate; remaining 4 failures split across DPLL hardware and infra flakes.
9 failed tests
TEST MEDIUM
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Cluster log-stream collapse + API auth expiry wiped 22 of 24 tests; a linuxptp-daemon state-machine regression caused the remaining 2 TGM holdover failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 12/24 failures — linuxptp-daemon log stream unreachable (ClockSync GetProfileLogID/GetClockIDForeign timeout): All ClockSync tests in bc (3), dualfollower (3), dualnicbc (3), dualnicbcha (2), and oc-serial test 1 (1) hit could not find regex in log stream … error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded or timedout waiting for matches. The test helper opens a live kubectl logs --follow stream and waits for a ptp4l profile or sync line that never arrives within 3–10 minutes. Two compounding factors: (a) ptp4l may not be reaching SLAVE state cluster-wide (no grandmaster reachable or log format changed by PR #613), and (b) when the API server began degrading, streaming connections were silently severed rather than cleanly refused.

• 10/24 failures — Kubernetes API server Unauthorized mid-run (oc-serial tests 2–11): After the first oc-serial test completed at ~07:29 UTC, the kubeconfig credentials expired or the API server rejected them (Unauthorized / couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials). Every subsequent test aborted immediately at the DaemonSet readiness check (linuxptp-daemon DaemonSet must exist before readiness wait → Unauthorized). One variant also showed dial tcp 10.8.34.117:6443: connection refused, indicating the API server briefly went fully unreachable.

• 2/24 failures — TGM holdover state-machine regression (tgm suite): Both T-GM holdover tests fail deterministically: disabling GNSS causes ts2phcState to be forced to PTP_FREERUN (not PTP_HOLDOVER) when NoSourceTSCount == 2 in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/event/event.go:402–403, making ClockClass 7 unreachable. Test 1 exhausted its 3-minute poll; test 2 observed map[metric:248] (freerun) throughout the 10-second sampling window. This is a deterministic regression, not a flake.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites affected
GetProfileLogID / GetClockIDForeign log stream timeoutbc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, oc (test 1)
API server Unauthorized cascadeoc (tests 2–11)
GNSS → ClockClass 7 holdover state-machine bugtgm

The log-stream failures are 100% cross-suite (5 of 6 suites, identical error text, different pod names) — confirming a cluster-wide cause rather than a suite-specific issue. The oc Unauthorized failures are oc-specific but are a secondary consequence of the same cluster degradation event. The tgm failures are suite-specific and code-driven.


No suite is entirely clean. dualnicbcha is the only suite where the log-stream failure count is below its total suite size (2 of 2 failed; no "passing" baseline in that suite either).



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job immediately and monitor the PTP grandmaster. The log-stream and API-auth failures bear all hallmarks of a transient lab infrastructure event (grandmaster unreachable, credential rotation, API server instability on telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com around 2026-06-24 07:00–08:30 UTC). A clean re-run will confirm whether the 22 non-TGM failures are infra flakes.

2. Audit linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (0459bc0, "upstream-sync-2026-06-22") for ptp4l log-format changes. If the upstream sync altered the format of the Ptp4lConf: or selected best master clock log lines, the test regexes in ptphelper.go and pods.go will never match regardless of cluster health. Compare the ptp4l log output from a pre-PR-613 build against a post-PR-613 pod to confirm or rule out a log-format regression.

3. Fix the TGM holdover state machine in event.go:402–403. Remove or guard the unconditional ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN override when NoSourceTSCount == 2; instead, allow the state to transition to PTP_HOLDOVER so the existing ClockClass 7 branch at lines 459–461 and 430–433 becomes reachable. This is a deterministic code regression and will not self-heal with a re-run.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant — 22/24 failures) with an embedded test_regression (2/24 tgm failures from PR #613 state-machine change)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — the entire 24-test job failed; the cluster experienced mid-run auth collapse, and a code regression in the TGM holdover path is deterministic and will recur on every re-run.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 ClockSync tests failed because Kubernetes log streaming to linuxptp-daemon pods returned context deadline exceeded / timeout errors during the GetProfileLogID step, preventing the test from locating the ptp4l config log line needed to derive the foreign master clock ID.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Kubernetes pod log stream failure in GetProfileLogID (affects: all 3 tests — "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hrsj8 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=timedout waiting for matches


could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-fpgx7 container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From the detailed agent logs (prior identical run same day, pod linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg):

```

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 1 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-5mcqg container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptphelper.go: 123] GetProfileLogID attempt 2 failed for test-bc-master1_test-bc-master1: ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded, retrying...

[ptptesthelper.go: 68] GetClockIDForeign retry due to err: could not get any profile line, err=could not find regex in log stream ... err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Additionally, the API server was returning Unauthorized by 08:29 UTC (end of job), with the bc ClockSync tests running at 07:02–08:29 UTC — indicating progressive auth/credential degradation throughout the long 3.5-hour job:

```

error: You must be logged in to the server (Unauthorized)

E0624 08:29:47.118913 memcache.go:265] "Unhandled Error" err="couldn't get current server API group list: the server has asked for the client to provide credentials"

```

• Root Cause: The test helper GetProfileLogID in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go opens a live Kubernetes log stream (Follow: true, no TailLines) via pods.GetPodLogsRegex searching for the regex (?m).?Ptp4lConf: #profile: (.|\n)?message_tag \[([^\]]*)\] within a 3-minute timeout. The stream — opened with context.TODO() so no test-side deadline — is being terminated by the infrastructure with context deadline exceeded before the regex can be matched. This error pattern repeats identically across all ClockSync tests in bc, dualfollower, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites, confirming a cluster-wide log streaming connectivity issue rather than a daemon log format regression. The linuxptp-daemon does emit this log line (printNodeProfile in pkg/daemon/daemon.go:1369 writes the rendered Ptp4lConf with #profile: prefix and message_tag), but the HTTP connection carrying the pod log stream is dropped before the data reaches the test. The progressive Unauthorized failures at job end suggest the kubeconfig token was near expiry, which may also have disrupted API server proxy connections to node kubelets mid-run.
• Breaking PR/Commit:


TL;DR: Five deterministic TBC regressions from PR #205 dominate; remaining 4 failures split across DPLL hardware and infra flakes.



Root Cause Groups:


• 5/9 failures — TBC test-code regression (PR #205, aede1e9, Jun 23 2026) — [tbc-serial] suite only. Two sub-causes, both introduced in the same commit:
• Profile discovery nil / log-stream timeout (2 failures): testconfig returns nil for release-4.22's naming convention → ptpConfig is nil panic and regex never matches daemon logs → context deadline exceeded.
• pmc exec split-argument bug (1+ failures): getClockIDViaPMC passes "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" as two separate exec-slice elements; pmc rejects each as bad command, so getClockIDViaPMC times out after 203 s and the priority-profile test never gets a foreign clock ID.
• (The TBC suite TL;DR also attributes failures to PR #618 phc2sys delayed-startup backport interacting with these test bugs, potentially accounting for remaining tbc failures.)

• 2/9 failures — T-GM DPLL skips HOLDOVER, stays at clock class 248 — [tgm-serial] suite only. After GNSS disruption the WPC T-GM DPLL transitions directly to FREERUN/INVALID instead of HOLDOVER, so updateGMState() never sets clockClass = 7. Could be a release-4.22 DPLL driver regression (candidate: PR #617 / fd6ff1e upstream-sync-2026-06-30) or a lab hardware/firmware issue on the T-GM node.

• 2/9 failures — Transient infra flakes — one per suite (bc, dualnicbcha):
• bc: Prometheus cadvisor query returns no valid data after 3 retries for master3.
• dualnicbcha: Kubernetes log-stream returns context deadline exceeded before phc2sys HA failover regex is matched. (Same error signature also appears in tbc, reinforcing the infra-blip explanation for those log-stream timeouts.)


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites
context deadline exceeded log-stream timeoutdualnicbcha, tbc (and possibly bc indirectly)
TBC test-code bugs (PR #205)tbc only — suite-specific regression
DPLL FREERUN instead of HOLDOVERtgm only — suite-specific
Prometheus unavailabilitybc only — suite-specific flake

The context deadline exceeded pattern crosses dualnicbcha and tbc, but in tbc it is at least partially caused by PR #205's broken regex derivation (no match → timeout), while in dualnicbcha it appears to be a pure timing/infra blip.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix PR #205 TBC test bugs (highest ROI — unblocks 5/9 failures):
• In test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go getClockIDViaPMC, join "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" into a single string argument: []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} (or the correct single-token form that pmc accepts).
• In test/pkg/testconfig/testconfig.go, add a nil-guard that skips TBC tests when no matching ptpConfig is discovered under release-4.22's naming convention, rather than proceeding to a failing Expect.

2. Investigate the T-GM DPLL HOLDOVER regression (unblocks 2/9 failures):
• Compare release-4.22 linuxptp-daemon DPLL driver commits since the last passing run (focus on dpll.go state-machine transitions around DPLL_FREERUN / DPLL_HOLDOVER).
• If no code change is found, re-run the job on the same T-GM lab node to determine whether this is a persistent hardware/firmware issue.

3. Harden retry logic for transient flakes (prevents future bc/dualnicbcha noise):
• Increase prometheusQueryRetries from 3 → 10 and the retry interval from 10 s → 30 s in testPtpCpuUtilization.
• Wrap the log-stream read in the phc2sys HA failover test with a retry loop (or increase its context timeout) to tolerate brief API-server connection blips.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — 5 of 9 failures are deterministic regressions blocking the entire TBC serial suite on every run; 2 more are potentially a software regression in the DPLL driver affecting T-GM holdover validation.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single bc-parallel failure is a transient Prometheus query timeout — the CPU utilization soak test exhausted all 3 retries trying to fetch cadvisor metrics for master3 from the in-cluster Prometheus pod, which returned no valid data.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Prometheus query returned no valid response after all retries (affects: [It] [bc-parallel] Soak testing PTP CPU Utilization)
• Evidence:

```

CPU usage query failed after 3 attempts: failed to get total cpu usage for ptp pods on node

master3.ptpcimno.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com: prometheus query failure: failed to get a

(valid) response from prometheus

```

(junit-failures.txt, lines 1–7)

• Root Cause: testPtpCpuUtilization (test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go) calls RetryCpuUsageCheck with prometheusQueryRetries=3 and a pkg.Timeout10Seconds retry interval. Each attempt calls isCpuUsageThresholdReachedInPtpPods → GetPodTotalCpuUsage → RunPrometheusQueryWithRetries (test/pkg/metrics/prometheus.go), which runs curl -s http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query inside the Prometheus pod via exec. All three outer retries failed for master3, ultimately returning fmt.Errorf("CPU usage query failed after %d attempts: %w", retries, queryErr). The most common causes are: Prometheus not yet having cadvisor scrape data for that node's new pods, a brief Prometheus pod restart, or an exec timeout. The node itself was Ready throughout the run (confirmed in build log: [INFO] All 6 nodes are Ready).
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no code change in main or release-4.22 is implicated; the failure is infrastructure-transient.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient Prometheus unavailability / missing cadvisor samples is a well-known intermittent condition in this suite.
• Suggested Fix: Increase prometheusQueryRetries in testPtpCpuUtilization from 3 to at least 5–10, and/or lengthen the retryInterval from pkg.Timeout10Seconds to 30 s so the cadvisor scrape has time to populate between attempts (test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go).

Suite Issue Class: test_flake

Suite Severity: LOW


dualnicbcha



Suite: dualnicbcha (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single DualNICBCHA serial test for phc2sys HA failover timed out waiting for the daemon to emit a failover log line, with the Kubernetes log stream returning context deadline exceeded before the expected regex was matched.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Log stream context deadline exceeded waiting for phc2sys HA failover log (affects: [It] [dualnicbcha-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync DualNICBCHA phc2sys switches to secondary ptp4l when primary interface fails [Serial])
• Evidence:

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-lnqk9

container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From junit-failures.txt line 11-15. The tbc-serial suite shows the identical error pattern on a different pod (linuxptp-daemon-dm54v) in the same run:

```

could not get slave profile name, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-dm54v container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

• Root Cause: The test streams linuxptp-daemon-container logs with a bounded Go context and waits for a regex confirming the phc2sys HA profile switch (the daemon emits ptp_ha_profile state N lines — see daemon.go:2161-2163 — derived from phc2sys output selecting as domain source clock / as out-of-domain source clock, constants HAInDomainIndicator/HAOutOfDomainIndicator at daemon.go:70-71). Before that regex appeared, the context deadline expired, causing the Kubernetes log-streaming API to return context deadline exceeded. The same failure signature on two independent pods (lnqk9, dm54v) in the same job run indicates the phc2sys failover was either slower than the per-test context timeout on the physical test hardware (cnfdf30-32, rdu2.redhat.com) or the log stream connection was intermittently degraded on the CI cluster.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the local linuxptp-daemon clone only has main branch with a single visible commit (fd6ff1e upstream sync 2026-06-30); release-4.22 branch is not available locally to diff. No code change can be confirmed as causal without inspecting the release-4.22 image.
• Is it a flake? yes — physical NIC interface-failure tests are inherently timing-sensitive; the identical context deadline exceeded log-stream error appearing across two independent suites (dualnicbcha and tbc) in the same run, without any corresponding infra crash or image-build failure, is characteristic of a transient hardware or API-server connection blip rather than a deterministic code regression.
• Suggested Fix: Re-run the job to confirm flakiness. If it recurs consistently, either increase the per-test log-stream context timeout for the phc2sys switches to secondary step, or add a retry loop around the log-stream read in the test helper so a single dropped connection does not immediately fail the test.

Suite Issue Class: test_flake

Suite Severity: MEDIUM


tbc



Suite: tbc (5 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 5 TBC serial failures stem from two newly-merged changes whose effects collide on release-4.22: a test-code bug in the brand-new TBC test suite (PR #205, Jun 23), and an incompatibility with the phc2sys delayed-startup feature backported to release-4.22 linuxptp-daemon (PR #618, Jun 30).



Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: TBC test discovery / log-stream failures (affects: "PTP daemon apply match rule based on nodeLabel", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master")
• Evidence:
• Test 1: could not get slave profile name, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-dm54v container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded
• Test 2: ptpConfig is nil { msg: "ptpConfig is nil", stack: [...] }
• Both tests are tagged [tbc-serial] and were added exclusively by ptp-operator commit aede1e9 ("Merge pull request #205 from nocturnalastro/tbc_ci_tests", Jun 23 2026), which introduced test/pkg/pmc/pmc.go (+447 lines) and +424 lines in test/conformance/serial/ptp.go
• Root Cause: The new TBC test code expects to discover a PTP profile via fullConfig.DiscoveredClockUnderTestPtpConfig.Name and then find a regex matching _tbc-tr or _tbc-tt in the daemon container log stream. The release-4.22 daemon images use a different profile naming/label convention, so the testconfig discovery (test/pkg/testconfig/testconfig.go +404 lines in the same PR) returns nil, causing test 2's ptpConfig is nil failure; test 1's log stream times out because the derived regex never matches actual daemon output.
• Breaking PR/Commit: ptp-operator aede1e9 / PR #205 (nocturnalastro/tbc_ci_tests), merged Jun 23 2026 — main-branch test code, no cherry-pick needed
• Is it a flake? No — deterministic discovery failure on every run
• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/testconfig/testconfig.go, add a fallback discovery path for release-4.22's profile naming convention, or add an explicit nil-guard + skip when no TBC ptpConfig is found rather than proceeding to a failing Expect.


• Group 2: pmc exec invocation bug in getClockIDViaPMC (affects: "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:
• Timed out after 203.289s. Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig temp
• parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.0.config: bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET
• Source of the error: test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go, function getClockIDViaPMC, line 144 (added in the same PR #205):

```go

buf, _, err := pods.ExecCommand(client.Client, true, pod,

pkg.PtpContainerName, []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"})

```

• "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" are passed as two separate elements in the exec args slice; pmc processes each remaining argument as an independent command and returns bad command: GET (GET is not a complete command without a TLV type) and bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET (not a valid pmc action).
• getClockIDViaPMC is called from GetClockIDForeign after log-parsing fallback (line 209), which is used by the "higher priority" test to verify the foreign clock ID.
• Root Cause: getClockIDViaPMC (introduced in aede1e9) splits the pmc command across two array elements. The Kubernetes exec API passes each element as a separate argv token, so the linuxptp pmc binary in the container receives "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" individually, neither of which is a valid standalone command. The correct invocation is []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} (single combined string), matching how the daemon-side pmc.go invokes it.
• Breaking PR/Commit: ptp-operator aede1e9 / PR #205, merged Jun 23 2026 — test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go line 144
• Is it a flake? No — deterministic on every invocation
• Suggested Fix: Change the getClockIDViaPMC call to pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single string argument: []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}.


• Group 3: phc2sys delayed-startup incompatibility (affects: "Should restart phc2sys with no socket errors after SIGTERM", "Check GM announcements in locked mode and holdover transitions")
• Evidence:
• Test 4: Timed out after 180.000s. Expected phc2sys process status to be 1 for GM. Expected : 0 to equal : 1
• Test 5: Timed out after 300.001s. Clock should reach holdover (class 135) with matching data. transitions=[6]. Expected : false to be true
• linuxptp-daemon commit d2ff2d1 "Close T-GM and T-BC phc2sys clock synchronization race conditions" (OCPBUGS-85586), merged to release-4.22 as PR #618 on Jun 30 2026 — two weeks after the TBC tests were written:

> "Synchronizing the system clock too early can lead to large time offsets that settle slowly. By delaying phc2sys from running until the PHC is 'reasonably synchronized' (ie within 1.0s offset of a trusted timesource) … In GM, we delay phc2sys until ts2phc offset is <1.0s."

• pkg/daemon/daemon.go: phc2sys is given skipInitialStartup = "waiting for PHC synchronization before adjusting system time" at profile-apply time and only started once HandleDelayedPhc2sysStartup fires (offset < 1 000 000 000 ns). Until that threshold is crossed, phc2sys cmdRun is never launched (continue in the startup loop at daemon.go:859–863).
• Test 4 is explicitly checking phc2sys process status … for GM — on the GM node phc2sys is now gated behind ts2phc reaching sub-second offset, so if the GNSS lock is slow or the test runs before the threshold is crossed, phc2sys count = 0.
• Test 5 saw 6 clock state transitions but never class 135; the phc2sys delay changes the timing of the ts2phc→phc2sys handoff, potentially disrupting the holdover sequence.
• The test-side fix commit bb43e35 ("test: update CI tests for delayed phc2sys startup", Jun 23 2026) patched DualNICBCHA explicitly but did not add equivalent guards for the TBC SIGTERM and holdover tests.
• Root Cause: linuxptp-daemon release-4.22 PR #618 (Jun 30 2026) delayed phc2sys startup for T-BC and T-GM profiles. The TBC test suite (merged Jun 23, one week earlier) was not updated to wait for


TL;DR: Five deterministic TBC regressions from PR #205 dominate; remaining 4 failures split across DPLL hardware and infra flakes.



Root Cause Groups:


• 5/9 failures — TBC test-code regression (PR #205, aede1e9, Jun 23 2026) — [tbc-serial] suite only. Two sub-causes, both introduced in the same commit:
• Profile discovery nil / log-stream timeout (2 failures): testconfig returns nil for release-4.22's naming convention → ptpConfig is nil panic and regex never matches daemon logs → context deadline exceeded.
• pmc exec split-argument bug (1+ failures): getClockIDViaPMC passes "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" as two separate exec-slice elements; pmc rejects each as bad command, so getClockIDViaPMC times out after 203 s and the priority-profile test never gets a foreign clock ID.
• (The TBC suite TL;DR also attributes failures to PR #618 phc2sys delayed-startup backport interacting with these test bugs, potentially accounting for remaining tbc failures.)

• 2/9 failures — T-GM DPLL skips HOLDOVER, stays at clock class 248 — [tgm-serial] suite only. After GNSS disruption the WPC T-GM DPLL transitions directly to FREERUN/INVALID instead of HOLDOVER, so updateGMState() never sets clockClass = 7. Could be a release-4.22 DPLL driver regression (candidate: PR #617 / fd6ff1e upstream-sync-2026-06-30) or a lab hardware/firmware issue on the T-GM node.

• 2/9 failures — Transient infra flakes — one per suite (bc, dualnicbcha):
• bc: Prometheus cadvisor query returns no valid data after 3 retries for master3.
• dualnicbcha: Kubernetes log-stream returns context deadline exceeded before phc2sys HA failover regex is matched. (Same error signature also appears in tbc, reinforcing the infra-blip explanation for those log-stream timeouts.)


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites
context deadline exceeded log-stream timeoutdualnicbcha, tbc (and possibly bc indirectly)
TBC test-code bugs (PR #205)tbc only — suite-specific regression
DPLL FREERUN instead of HOLDOVERtgm only — suite-specific
Prometheus unavailabilitybc only — suite-specific flake

The context deadline exceeded pattern crosses dualnicbcha and tbc, but in tbc it is at least partially caused by PR #205's broken regex derivation (no match → timeout), while in dualnicbcha it appears to be a pure timing/infra blip.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix PR #205 TBC test bugs (highest ROI — unblocks 5/9 failures):
• In test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go getClockIDViaPMC, join "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" into a single string argument: []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} (or the correct single-token form that pmc accepts).
• In test/pkg/testconfig/testconfig.go, add a nil-guard that skips TBC tests when no matching ptpConfig is discovered under release-4.22's naming convention, rather than proceeding to a failing Expect.

2. Investigate the T-GM DPLL HOLDOVER regression (unblocks 2/9 failures):
• Compare release-4.22 linuxptp-daemon DPLL driver commits since the last passing run (focus on dpll.go state-machine transitions around DPLL_FREERUN / DPLL_HOLDOVER).
• If no code change is found, re-run the job on the same T-GM lab node to determine whether this is a persistent hardware/firmware issue.

3. Harden retry logic for transient flakes (prevents future bc/dualnicbcha noise):
• Increase prometheusQueryRetries from 3 → 10 and the retry interval from 10 s → 30 s in testPtpCpuUtilization.
• Wrap the log-stream read in the phc2sys HA failover test with a retry loop (or increase its context timeout) to tolerate brief API-server connection blips.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — 5 of 9 failures are deterministic regressions blocking the entire TBC serial suite on every run; 2 more are potentially a software regression in the DPLL driver affecting T-GM holdover validation.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single bc-parallel failure is a transient Prometheus query timeout — the CPU utilization soak test exhausted all 3 retries trying to fetch cadvisor metrics for master3 from the in-cluster Prometheus pod, which returned no valid data.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Prometheus query returned no valid response after all retries (affects: [It] [bc-parallel] Soak testing PTP CPU Utilization)
• Evidence:

```

CPU usage query failed after 3 attempts: failed to get total cpu usage for ptp pods on node

master3.ptpcimno.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com: prometheus query failure: failed to get a

(valid) response from prometheus

```

(junit-failures.txt, lines 1–7)

• Root Cause: testPtpCpuUtilization (test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go) calls RetryCpuUsageCheck with prometheusQueryRetries=3 and a pkg.Timeout10Seconds retry interval. Each attempt calls isCpuUsageThresholdReachedInPtpPods → GetPodTotalCpuUsage → RunPrometheusQueryWithRetries (test/pkg/metrics/prometheus.go), which runs curl -s http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query inside the Prometheus pod via exec. All three outer retries failed for master3, ultimately returning fmt.Errorf("CPU usage query failed after %d attempts: %w", retries, queryErr). The most common causes are: Prometheus not yet having cadvisor scrape data for that node's new pods, a brief Prometheus pod restart, or an exec timeout. The node itself was Ready throughout the run (confirmed in build log: [INFO] All 6 nodes are Ready).
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no code change in main or release-4.22 is implicated; the failure is infrastructure-transient.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient Prometheus unavailability / missing cadvisor samples is a well-known intermittent condition in this suite.
• Suggested Fix: Increase prometheusQueryRetries in testPtpCpuUtilization from 3 to at least 5–10, and/or lengthen the retryInterval from pkg.Timeout10Seconds to 30 s so the cadvisor scrape has time to populate between attempts (test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go).

Suite Issue Class: test_flake

Suite Severity: LOW


dualnicbcha



Suite: dualnicbcha (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single DualNICBCHA serial test for phc2sys HA failover timed out waiting for the daemon to emit a failover log line, with the Kubernetes log stream returning context deadline exceeded before the expected regex was matched.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Log stream context deadline exceeded waiting for phc2sys HA failover log (affects: [It] [dualnicbcha-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP ClockSync DualNICBCHA phc2sys switches to secondary ptp4l when primary interface fails [Serial])
• Evidence:

```

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-lnqk9

container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

From junit-failures.txt line 11-15. The tbc-serial suite shows the identical error pattern on a different pod (linuxptp-daemon-dm54v) in the same run:

```

could not get slave profile name, err=could not find regex in log stream for

openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-dm54v container=linuxptp-daemon-container,

err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

• Root Cause: The test streams linuxptp-daemon-container logs with a bounded Go context and waits for a regex confirming the phc2sys HA profile switch (the daemon emits ptp_ha_profile state N lines — see daemon.go:2161-2163 — derived from phc2sys output selecting as domain source clock / as out-of-domain source clock, constants HAInDomainIndicator/HAOutOfDomainIndicator at daemon.go:70-71). Before that regex appeared, the context deadline expired, causing the Kubernetes log-streaming API to return context deadline exceeded. The same failure signature on two independent pods (lnqk9, dm54v) in the same job run indicates the phc2sys failover was either slower than the per-test context timeout on the physical test hardware (cnfdf30-32, rdu2.redhat.com) or the log stream connection was intermittently degraded on the CI cluster.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — the local linuxptp-daemon clone only has main branch with a single visible commit (fd6ff1e upstream sync 2026-06-30); release-4.22 branch is not available locally to diff. No code change can be confirmed as causal without inspecting the release-4.22 image.
• Is it a flake? yes — physical NIC interface-failure tests are inherently timing-sensitive; the identical context deadline exceeded log-stream error appearing across two independent suites (dualnicbcha and tbc) in the same run, without any corresponding infra crash or image-build failure, is characteristic of a transient hardware or API-server connection blip rather than a deterministic code regression.
• Suggested Fix: Re-run the job to confirm flakiness. If it recurs consistently, either increase the per-test log-stream context timeout for the phc2sys switches to secondary step, or add a retry loop around the log-stream read in the test helper so a single dropped connection does not immediately fail the test.

Suite Issue Class: test_flake

Suite Severity: MEDIUM


tbc



Suite: tbc (5 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 5 TBC serial failures stem from two newly-merged changes whose effects collide on release-4.22: a test-code bug in the brand-new TBC test suite (PR #205, Jun 23), and an incompatibility with the phc2sys delayed-startup feature backported to release-4.22 linuxptp-daemon (PR #618, Jun 30).



Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: TBC test discovery / log-stream failures (affects: "PTP daemon apply match rule based on nodeLabel", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master")
• Evidence:
• Test 1: could not get slave profile name, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-dm54v container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded
• Test 2: ptpConfig is nil { msg: "ptpConfig is nil", stack: [...] }
• Both tests are tagged [tbc-serial] and were added exclusively by ptp-operator commit aede1e9 ("Merge pull request #205 from nocturnalastro/tbc_ci_tests", Jun 23 2026), which introduced test/pkg/pmc/pmc.go (+447 lines) and +424 lines in test/conformance/serial/ptp.go
• Root Cause: The new TBC test code expects to discover a PTP profile via fullConfig.DiscoveredClockUnderTestPtpConfig.Name and then find a regex matching _tbc-tr or _tbc-tt in the daemon container log stream. The release-4.22 daemon images use a different profile naming/label convention, so the testconfig discovery (test/pkg/testconfig/testconfig.go +404 lines in the same PR) returns nil, causing test 2's ptpConfig is nil failure; test 1's log stream times out because the derived regex never matches actual daemon output.
• Breaking PR/Commit: ptp-operator aede1e9 / PR #205 (nocturnalastro/tbc_ci_tests), merged Jun 23 2026 — main-branch test code, no cherry-pick needed
• Is it a flake? No — deterministic discovery failure on every run
• Suggested Fix: In test/pkg/testconfig/testconfig.go, add a fallback discovery path for release-4.22's profile naming convention, or add an explicit nil-guard + skip when no TBC ptpConfig is found rather than proceeding to a failing Expect.


• Group 2: pmc exec invocation bug in getClockIDViaPMC (affects: "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:
• Timed out after 203.289s. Timeout to get foreign clock ID for ptpconfig temp
• parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.0.config: bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET
• Source of the error: test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go, function getClockIDViaPMC, line 144 (added in the same PR #205):

```go

buf, _, err := pods.ExecCommand(client.Client, true, pod,

pkg.PtpContainerName, []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"})

```

• "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" are passed as two separate elements in the exec args slice; pmc processes each remaining argument as an independent command and returns bad command: GET (GET is not a complete command without a TLV type) and bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET (not a valid pmc action).
• getClockIDViaPMC is called from GetClockIDForeign after log-parsing fallback (line 209), which is used by the "higher priority" test to verify the foreign clock ID.
• Root Cause: getClockIDViaPMC (introduced in aede1e9) splits the pmc command across two array elements. The Kubernetes exec API passes each element as a separate argv token, so the linuxptp pmc binary in the container receives "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" individually, neither of which is a valid standalone command. The correct invocation is []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} (single combined string), matching how the daemon-side pmc.go invokes it.
• Breaking PR/Commit: ptp-operator aede1e9 / PR #205, merged Jun 23 2026 — test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go line 144
• Is it a flake? No — deterministic on every invocation
• Suggested Fix: Change the getClockIDViaPMC call to pass "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single string argument: []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}.


• Group 3: phc2sys delayed-startup incompatibility (affects: "Should restart phc2sys with no socket errors after SIGTERM", "Check GM announcements in locked mode and holdover transitions")
• Evidence:
• Test 4: Timed out after 180.000s. Expected phc2sys process status to be 1 for GM. Expected : 0 to equal : 1
• Test 5: Timed out after 300.001s. Clock should reach holdover (class 135) with matching data. transitions=[6]. Expected : false to be true
• linuxptp-daemon commit d2ff2d1 "Close T-GM and T-BC phc2sys clock synchronization race conditions" (OCPBUGS-85586), merged to release-4.22 as PR #618 on Jun 30 2026 — two weeks after the TBC tests were written:

> "Synchronizing the system clock too early can lead to large time offsets that settle slowly. By delaying phc2sys from running until the PHC is 'reasonably synchronized' (ie within 1.0s offset of a trusted timesource) … In GM, we delay phc2sys until ts2phc offset is <1.0s."

• pkg/daemon/daemon.go: phc2sys is given skipInitialStartup = "waiting for PHC synchronization before adjusting system time" at profile-apply time and only started once HandleDelayedPhc2sysStartup fires (offset < 1 000 000 000 ns). Until that threshold is crossed, phc2sys cmdRun is never launched (continue in the startup loop at daemon.go:859–863).
• Test 4 is explicitly checking phc2sys process status … for GM — on the GM node phc2sys is now gated behind ts2phc reaching sub-second offset, so if the GNSS lock is slow or the test runs before the threshold is crossed, phc2sys count = 0.
• Test 5 saw 6 clock state transitions but never class 135; the phc2sys delay changes the timing of the ts2phc→phc2sys handoff, potentially disrupting the holdover sequence.
• The test-side fix commit bb43e35 ("test: update CI tests for delayed phc2sys startup", Jun 23 2026) patched DualNICBCHA explicitly but did not add equivalent guards for the TBC SIGTERM and holdover tests.
• Root Cause: linuxptp-daemon release-4.22 PR #618 (Jun 30 2026) delayed phc2sys startup for T-BC and T-GM profiles. The TBC test suite (merged Jun 23, one week earlier) was not updated to wait for


TL;DR: Five deterministic TBC regressions from PR #205 dominate; remaining 4 failures split across DPLL hardware and infra flakes.



Root Cause Groups:


• 5/9 failures — TBC test-code regression (PR #205, aede1e9, Jun 23 2026) — [tbc-serial] suite only. Two sub-causes, both introduced in the same commit:
• Profile discovery nil / log-stream timeout (2 failures): testconfig returns nil for release-4.22's naming convention → ptpConfig is nil panic and regex never matches daemon logs → context deadline exceeded.
• pmc exec split-argument bug (1+ failures): getClockIDViaPMC passes "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" as two separate exec-slice elements; pmc rejects each as bad command, so getClockIDViaPMC times out after 203 s and the priority-profile test never gets a foreign clock ID.
• (The TBC suite TL;DR also attributes failures to PR #618 phc2sys delayed-startup backport interacting with these test bugs, potentially accounting for remaining tbc failures.)

• 2/9 failures — T-GM DPLL skips HOLDOVER, stays at clock class 248 — [tgm-serial] suite only. After GNSS disruption the WPC T-GM DPLL transitions directly to FREERUN/INVALID instead of HOLDOVER, so updateGMState() never sets clockClass = 7. Could be a release-4.22 DPLL driver regression (candidate: PR #617 / fd6ff1e upstream-sync-2026-06-30) or a lab hardware/firmware issue on the T-GM node.

• 2/9 failures — Transient infra flakes — one per suite (bc, dualnicbcha):
• bc: Prometheus cadvisor query returns no valid data after 3 retries for master3.
• dualnicbcha: Kubernetes log-stream returns context deadline exceeded before phc2sys HA failover regex is matched. (Same error signature also appears in tbc, reinforcing the infra-blip explanation for those log-stream timeouts.)


Cross-Suite Patterns:


PatternSuites
context deadline exceeded log-stream timeoutdualnicbcha, tbc (and possibly bc indirectly)
TBC test-code bugs (PR #205)tbc only — suite-specific regression
DPLL FREERUN instead of HOLDOVERtgm only — suite-specific
Prometheus unavailabilitybc only — suite-specific flake

The context deadline exceeded pattern crosses dualnicbcha and tbc, but in tbc it is at least partially caused by PR #205's broken regex derivation (no match → timeout), while in dualnicbcha it appears to be a pure timing/infra blip.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix PR #205 TBC test bugs (highest ROI — unblocks 5/9 failures):
• In test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go getClockIDViaPMC, join "GET" and "PARENT_DATA_SET" into a single string argument: []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} (or the correct single-token form that pmc accepts).
• In test/pkg/testconfig/testconfig.go, add a nil-guard that skips TBC tests when no matching ptpConfig is discovered under release-4.22's naming convention, rather than proceeding to a failing Expect.

2. Investigate the T-GM DPLL HOLDOVER regression (unblocks 2/9 failures):
• Compare release-4.22 linuxptp-daemon DPLL driver commits since the last passing run (focus on dpll.go state-machine transitions around DPLL_FREERUN / DPLL_HOLDOVER).
• If no code change is found, re-run the job on the same T-GM lab node to determine whether this is a persistent hardware/firmware issue.

3. Harden retry logic for transient flakes (prevents future bc/dualnicbcha noise):
• Increase prometheusQueryRetries from 3 → 10 and the retry interval from 10 s → 30 s in testPtpCpuUtilization.
• Wrap the log-stream read in the phc2sys HA failover test with a retry loop (or increase its context timeout) to tolerate brief API-server connection blips.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression


Overall Severity: HIGH — 5 of 9 failures are deterministic regressions blocking the entire TBC serial suite on every run; 2 more are potentially a software regression in the DPLL driver affecting T-GM holdover validation.




Per-Suite Details



bc



Suite: bc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The single bc-parallel failure is a transient Prometheus query timeout — the CPU utilization soak test exhausted all 3 retries trying to fetch cadvisor metrics for master3 from the in-cluster Prometheus pod, which returned no valid data.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Prometheus query returned no valid response after all retries (affects: [It] [bc-parallel] Soak testing PTP CPU Utilization)
• Evidence:

```

CPU usage query failed after 3 attempts: failed to get total cpu usage for ptp pods on node

master3.ptpcimno.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com: prometheus query failure: failed to get a

(valid) response from prometheus

```

(junit-failures.txt, lines 1–7)

• Root Cause: testPtpCpuUtilization (test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go) calls RetryCpuUsageCheck with prometheusQueryRetries=3 and a pkg.Timeout10Seconds retry interval. Each attempt calls isCpuUsageThresholdReachedInPtpPods → GetPodTotalCpuUsage → RunPrometheusQueryWithRetries (test/pkg/metrics/prometheus.go), which runs curl -s http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query inside the Prometheus pod via exec. All three outer retries failed for master3, ultimately returning fmt.Errorf("CPU usage query failed after %d attempts: %w", retries, queryErr). The most common causes are: Prometheus not yet having cadvisor scrape data for that node's new pods, a brief Prometheus pod restart, or an exec timeout. The node itself was Ready throughout the run (confirmed in build log: [INFO] All 6 nodes are Ready).
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — no code change in main or release-4.22 is implicated; the failure is infrastructure-transient.
• Is it a flake? Yes — transient Prometheus unavailability / missing cadvisor samples is a well-known intermittent condition in this suite.
• Suggested Fix: Increase prometheusQueryRetries in testPtpCpuUtilization from 3 to at least 5–10, and/or lengthen the retryInterval from pkg.Timeout10Seconds to 30 s so the cadvisor scrape has time to populate between attempts (test/conformance/parallel/ptp.go).

Suite Issue Class: test_flake

Suite Severity: LOW


dualnicbcha

```
container=linuxptp-daemon-container, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

```

Before that regex appeared, the context deadline expired, causing the Kubernetes log-streaming API to return context deadline exceeded. If it recurs consistently, either increase the per-test log-stream context timeout for the phc2sys switches to secondary step, or add a retry loop around the log-stream read in the test helper so a single dropped connection does not immediately fail the test.

• Test 5: Timed out after 300.001s. Clock should reach holdover (class 135) with matching data. transitions=[6]. Expected : false to be true
