```

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node cnfdf31's follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and ens2f3np3 was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Tests 1 & 2 — identical error

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available op[tions: ...]

```

```

Test 3 — additional symptom

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found

```

```

Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

```

tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)

...clock class: 248 (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)

```

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.

Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).

This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.

Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node cnfdf31's follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and ens2f3np3 was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Tests 1 & 2 — identical error

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available op[tions: ...]

```

```

Test 3 — additional symptom

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found

```

```

Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

```

tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)

...clock class: 248 (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)

```

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower

Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

available op[tions: ...]

```

Test 3 — additional symptom

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found

```

```

Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

```

tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)

...clock class: 248 (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)

```

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.

pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.

Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.

• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node cnfdf31's follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and ens2f3np3 was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Tests 1 & 2 — identical error

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available op[tions: ...]

```

```

Test 3 — additional symptom

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found

```

```

Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

```

tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)

...clock class: 248 (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)

```

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node cnfdf31's follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and ens2f3np3 was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).

Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 32 | Suites: dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm



TL;DR: All 32 failures trace to a single unlocked T-GM (ts2phc FREERUN) and a broken pmc command interface cascading across every suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — T-GM ts2phc stuck in FREERUN (clockClass 248) while dpll=LOCKED (tgm: 10, dualnicbc: ~5, dualnicbcha: ~4): The GM node cnfdf32 never emits clockClass 6 because ts2phc_parser.go accumulates "source ts not valid" hits and forces ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN, overriding a confirmed dpll LOCK. This starves every downstream suite — BC slave ports on the dualnic nodes have no locked upstream grandmaster to sync to, so they remain MASTER/FREERUN themselves, cascading into all WPC GM state, holdover, recovery, and event-timeout failures across all three suites.

• ~13/32 failures — pmc rejects GET PARENT_DATA_SET as two "bad command" tokens (dualnicbc: 5, dualnicbcha: 4, cross-suite overlap ~4): Tests calling ptptesthelper.go:106 via pmc interactive mode receive bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET for every query, blocking all clock-identity discovery. The two-token error pattern is diagnostic of either (a) the unpinned linuxptp RPM in Dockerfile.ocp updating to ≥4.2 where management_enabled=0 is the new default, or (b) a code path in pmc.go passing "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single unsplit argument to exec.Command. This blocks "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", and "higher priority" tests in both dualnic suites.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
T-GM FREERUN (ts2phc=0, dpll=1)Yes (BC slave has no upstream GM)Yes (BC slave has no upstream GM)Yes (primary — all WPC tests)
pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SETYesYesNo

• Shared across all suites: The absent/unlocked grandmaster. The tgm suite owns the root of this failure; dualnic suites are casualties of it.
• Shared across dualnicbc + dualnicbcha only: The pmc command-format breakage. Identical error strings, identical affected test names — this is one systemic defect, not two independent bugs.
• Suite-specific: No failures appear to be genuinely isolated to a single suite; all are manifestations of the two root causes above.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix the T-GM ts2phc FREERUN (highest leverage — unblocks ~19 failures across all suites): On node cnfdf32, determine whether a recent linuxptp update changed the ts2phc log format so that regularTS2PhcRegex in ts2phc_parser.go:14–23 no longer matches, causing silent NoSourceTSCount accumulation. If the regex is the cause, update it to match the new format. Also verify whether commit fd6ff1e (upstream-sync, merged 2026-06-30 on main) was cherry-picked to release-5.0 and introduced a regression in the ts2phc state machine.

2. Fix the pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET command breakage (unblocks ~13 failures in dualnic suites): Pin the linuxptp RPM to a known-working version in linuxptp-daemon/addons/redhat/Dockerfile.ocp (currently yum install linuxptp with no version). As a parallel fix, verify that pmc.go and ptptesthelper.go:106 pass GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate arguments to exec.Command rather than as a single space-delimited string. Additionally, explicitly set management_enabled 1 in the ptp4l config template in pkg/daemon/config.go to prevent linuxptp ≥4.2 from silently disabling management queries.

3. Investigate whether fd6ff1e needs a backport or revert on release-5.0: Run python scripts/check_backport.py /tmp/ci-investigate fd6ff1e release-5.0 (once available) to confirm whether the 2026-06-30 upstream-sync commit is present in the 5.0 image. If it introduced the ts2phc parser regression or the pmc argument bug, revert or cherry-pick the targeted fix to release-5.0 rather than carrying the full sync.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(Deterministic, reproducible across suites in a single run; not a flake, not an infra timeout — the metrics and log evidence point to a code/package change breaking both the GM state machine and the pmc interface.)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The T-GM never locks, meaning the entire PTP sync hierarchy is non-functional; 32/32 failures, all three suites affected, no tests pass in the sync-dependent path.)




Per-Suite Details



dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (10 failures)


Suite TL;DR: Every test in the dualnicbc suite fails because the Boundary Clock (BC) slave port ens2f0np0 never achieves SLAVE state (stuck at clockClass 248 / FREERUN), which cascades into pmc query failures, stale metrics, event timeouts, and outage-recovery timeouts; the T-GM's own ts2phc is also in FREERUN, indicating the root failure is the absence of a locked upstream grandmaster.



Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: pmc returns bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

grandmasterIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

Identical errors appear for ptp4l.0.config in the "higher priority" test, and identically in the dualnicbcha suite on the same node.

• Root Cause: ptptesthelper.go:106 spawns pmc -u -b 0 -f /var/run/ptp4l.N.config in interactive mode via goexpect and sends GET PARENT_DATA_SET\n (see linuxptp-daemon/pkg/pmc/pmc.go:17,49,147). The bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET output is pmc's own parser rejecting each token as an unrecognized command. This is consistent with a linuxptp version in the release-5.0 image where the pmc interactive-mode command table does not include GET / SET prefixes, or with management_enabled=0 on ptp4l (added in linuxptp ≥4.2 as the default) causing ptp4l to respond with NOT\_SUPPORTED which pmc renders as "bad command". The Dockerfile.ocp (linuxptp-daemon/addons/redhat/Dockerfile.ocp:12) installs linuxptp from RHEL9 repos with no version pin, so an unintended package update to ≥4.2 is a plausible trigger.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown – no pinned linuxptp version; the upstream-sync PR fd6ff1e ("CNF-22056/CNF-22693/CNF-25167/OCPBUGS-85586/OCPBUGS-93744", merged 2026-06-30 on main of linuxptp-daemon) is only on main and would not affect release-5.0 images unless cherry-picked. Verify with: python scripts/check_backport.py /tmp/ci-investigate fd6ff1e release-5.0
• Is it a flake? No – same error on both ptp4l.0.config and ptp4l.1.config, and reproduced identically in dualnicbcha on the same run.
• Suggested Fix: Pin linuxptp to a specific RPM version in Dockerfile.ocp, or explicitly add management_enabled 1 to the ptp4l config template in pkg/daemon/config.go so management queries work regardless of linuxptp version default.

• Group 2: BC slave port stuck as MASTER, clockClass 248 (FREERUN) (affects: "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source", "PTP Outage recovery: interface taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage")
• Evidence:

```

Expected PMC clock class 6 for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config

: 248 to equal : 6

incorrect role for ens2f0np0, role expected=1(SLAVE), role observed=2(MASTER)

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

: false to be true

```

From test discovery output:

```

INFO [testconfig.go: 2172] ptptConfig: test-bc-master1, master




Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 32 | Suites: dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm



TL;DR: All 32 failures trace to a single unlocked T-GM (ts2phc FREERUN) and a broken pmc command interface cascading across every suite.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — T-GM ts2phc stuck in FREERUN (clockClass 248) while dpll=LOCKED (tgm: 10, dualnicbc: ~5, dualnicbcha: ~4): The GM node cnfdf32 never emits clockClass 6 because ts2phc_parser.go accumulates "source ts not valid" hits and forces ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN, overriding a confirmed dpll LOCK. This starves every downstream suite — BC slave ports on the dualnic nodes have no locked upstream grandmaster to sync to, so they remain MASTER/FREERUN themselves, cascading into all WPC GM state, holdover, recovery, and event-timeout failures across all three suites.

• ~13/32 failures — pmc rejects GET PARENT_DATA_SET as two "bad command" tokens (dualnicbc: 5, dualnicbcha: 4, cross-suite overlap ~4): Tests calling ptptesthelper.go:106 via pmc interactive mode receive bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET for every query, blocking all clock-identity discovery. The two-token error pattern is diagnostic of either (a) the unpinned linuxptp RPM in Dockerfile.ocp updating to ≥4.2 where management_enabled=0 is the new default, or (b) a code path in pmc.go passing "GET PARENT_DATA_SET" as a single unsplit argument to exec.Command. This blocks "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", and "higher priority" tests in both dualnic suites.


Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
T-GM FREERUN (ts2phc=0, dpll=1)Yes (BC slave has no upstream GM)Yes (BC slave has no upstream GM)Yes (primary — all WPC tests)
pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SETYesYesNo

• Shared across all suites: The absent/unlocked grandmaster. The tgm suite owns the root of this failure; dualnic suites are casualties of it.
• Shared across dualnicbc + dualnicbcha only: The pmc command-format breakage. Identical error strings, identical affected test names — this is one systemic defect, not two independent bugs.
• Suite-specific: No failures appear to be genuinely isolated to a single suite; all are manifestations of the two root causes above.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Fix the T-GM ts2phc FREERUN (highest leverage — unblocks ~19 failures across all suites): On node cnfdf32, determine whether a recent linuxptp update changed the ts2phc log format so that regularTS2PhcRegex in ts2phc_parser.go:14–23 no longer matches, causing silent NoSourceTSCount accumulation. If the regex is the cause, update it to match the new format. Also verify whether commit fd6ff1e (upstream-sync, merged 2026-06-30 on main) was cherry-picked to release-5.0 and introduced a regression in the ts2phc state machine.

2. Fix the pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET command breakage (unblocks ~13 failures in dualnic suites): Pin the linuxptp RPM to a known-working version in linuxptp-daemon/addons/redhat/Dockerfile.ocp (currently yum install linuxptp with no version). As a parallel fix, verify that pmc.go and ptptesthelper.go:106 pass GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate arguments to exec.Command rather than as a single space-delimited string. Additionally, explicitly set management_enabled 1 in the ptp4l config template in pkg/daemon/config.go to prevent linuxptp ≥4.2 from silently disabling management queries.

3. Investigate whether fd6ff1e needs a backport or revert on release-5.0: Run python scripts/check_backport.py /tmp/ci-investigate fd6ff1e release-5.0 (once available) to confirm whether the 2026-06-30 upstream-sync commit is present in the 5.0 image. If it introduced the ts2phc parser regression or the pmc argument bug, revert or cherry-pick the targeted fix to release-5.0 rather than carrying the full sync.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(Deterministic, reproducible across suites in a single run; not a flake, not an infra timeout — the metrics and log evidence point to a code/package change breaking both the GM state machine and the pmc interface.)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The T-GM never locks, meaning the entire PTP sync hierarchy is non-functional; 32/32 failures, all three suites affected, no tests pass in the sync-dependent path.)




Per-Suite Details



dualnicbc



Suite: dualnicbc (10 failures)


Suite TL;DR: Every test in the dualnicbc suite fails because the Boundary Clock (BC) slave port ens2f0np0 never achieves SLAVE state (stuck at clockClass 248 / FREERUN), which cascades into pmc query failures, stale metrics, event timeouts, and outage-recovery timeouts; the T-GM's own ts2phc is also in FREERUN, indicating the root failure is the absence of a locked upstream grandmaster.



Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: pmc returns bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

grandmasterIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

Identical errors appear for ptp4l.0.config in the "higher priority" test, and identically in the dualnicbcha suite on the same node.

• Root Cause: ptptesthelper.go:106 spawns pmc -u -b 0 -f /var/run/ptp4l.N.config in interactive mode via goexpect and sends GET PARENT_DATA_SET\n (see linuxptp-daemon/pkg/pmc/pmc.go:17,49,147). The bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET output is pmc's own parser rejecting each token as an unrecognized command. This is consistent with a linuxptp version in the release-5.0 image where the pmc interactive-mode command table does not include GET / SET prefixes, or with management_enabled=0 on ptp4l (added in linuxptp ≥4.2 as the default) causing ptp4l to respond with NOT\_SUPPORTED which pmc renders as "bad command". The Dockerfile.ocp (linuxptp-daemon/addons/redhat/Dockerfile.ocp:12) installs linuxptp from RHEL9 repos with no version pin, so an unintended package update to ≥4.2 is a plausible trigger.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown – no pinned linuxptp version; the upstream-sync PR fd6ff1e ("CNF-22056/CNF-22693/CNF-25167/OCPBUGS-85586/OCPBUGS-93744", merged 2026-06-30 on main of linuxptp-daemon) is only on main and would not affect release-5.0 images unless cherry-picked. Verify with: python scripts/check_backport.py /tmp/ci-investigate fd6ff1e release-5.0
• Is it a flake? No – same error on both ptp4l.0.config and ptp4l.1.config, and reproduced identically in dualnicbcha on the same run.
• Suggested Fix: Pin linuxptp to a specific RPM version in Dockerfile.ocp, or explicitly add management_enabled 1 to the ptp4l config template in pkg/daemon/config.go so management queries work regardless of linuxptp version default.

• Group 2: BC slave port stuck as MASTER, clockClass 248 (FREERUN) (affects: "Verify clockClass when locking PTP source", "PTP Outage recovery: interface taken down and up", "BC clock class recovers to Locked after upstream link outage")
• Evidence:

```

Expected PMC clock class 6 for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config

: 248 to equal : 6

incorrect role for ens2f0np0, role expected=1(SLAVE), role observed=2(MASTER)

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

: false to be true

```

From test discovery output:

```

INFO [testconfig.go: 2172] ptptConfig: test-bc-master1, master



TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 3 dualfollower ClockSync tests timed out waiting for node cnfdf31's follower interfaces to reach LOCKED state because the openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 was never emitted by linuxptp-daemon, and ens2f3np3 was stuck in LISTENING rather than SLAVE role.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Slave node never locked — missing clock_state metric and port stuck in LISTENING (affects: all 3 tests)
• Evidence:

```

Tests 1 & 2 — identical error

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available op[tions: ...]

```

```

Test 3 — additional symptom

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig temp

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="",

slaveIfs=[ens2f3np3 ens2f0np0]). Per-interface results:

ens2f3np3: role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING)

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found

```

```

Broader context from other suites (junit-failures.txt)

parentPortIdentity.clockIdentity not found in pmc output for /var/run/ptp4l.1.config:

bad command: GET

bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET

```

```

tgm suite (junit-failures.txt)

Expected ptp4l clock class state to eventually be Locked (class 6)

...clock class: 248 (← default/FREERUN value, never reached 6)

```

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Likely pre-dates the fix in linuxptp-daemon PR #613 (OCPBUGS-90708 / CNF-24996, merged 2026-06-23, SHA 0459bc0) and cloud-event-proxy PR #701 (OCPBUGS-88369, merged 2026-06-25, SHA fc5d567) — both merged after this CI run on 2026-06-21. The breaking change introducing the regression is Unknown (upstream/downstream commit not visible in the shallow clone).
• Is it a flake? No — all four test suites in the same job (dualfollower, dualnicbc, dualnicbcha, tgm) failed with consistent, correlated errors. The T-GM staying at clock class 248 is a systematic fa


TL;DR: WPC T-GM grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock (class 248), collapsing all 4 suites; pmc argument bug added 5 independent failures.



Root Cause Groups:


• 20/25 failures — GM in FREERUN (clock class 248), cascading to all BC/slave/follower sync tests

Affects: tgm (5/5), dualnicbcha (~6/8), dualnicbc (~6/9), dualfollower (3/3). The WPC T-GM node hosting linuxptp-daemon-7g7z8 never transitioned out of FREERUN during the run. With no locked GM advertising Announce messages, the entire downstream chain — BC → dualnicbc → dualnicbcha → dualfollower slaves — could not lock. The tgm log stream stayed open but hit context deadline exceeded after 340 s without ever emitting a LOCKED event; all four state-based tgm tests timed out on Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM / : false. The dualfollower node cnfdf31 exhibits both missing openshift_ptp_clock_state metric for ens2f0np0 and ens2f3np3 stuck in LISTENING — consistent with no upstream Announce to trigger SLAVE election.


• 5/25 failures — pmc invoked with split arguments; GET PARENT_DATA_SET rejected as two bad commands

Affects: dualnicbc ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority") and dualnicbcha ("Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master"). pmc is called with GET and PARENT_DATA_SET as separate argv elements; the binary responds bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Identical verbatim error appears in both suites, ruling out a flake. Likely triggered by the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary (linuxptp ≥ 4.3, el10_2) changing batch-mode argument tokenization, or the test helper failing to quote the combined string. Other pmc queries (e.g. GET DEFAULT_DATA_SET) still succeed, confirming the socket and ptp4l process are reachable.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
GM FREERUN / GNSS never lockedAll 3~6~6All 5
pmc split-arg bug—32—

• The GM FREERUN failure is shared across all 4 suites — it is the single largest driver and the only failure in tgm and dualfollower.
• The pmc split-arg bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha only (suites that exercise PARENT_DATA_SET queries via the BC node).
• No failures are genuinely suite-specific; the dualfollower and tgm failures are pure downstream consequences of the shared GM not locking.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Re-run the job and inspect GM GNSS hardware first. Confirm whether the WPC T-GM node achieves GNSS/DPLL lock in a fresh run. If it fails again, check the GNSS antenna cable, receiver status, and ts2phc DPLL netlink events on that node. This single fix would resolve ~20/25 failures.

2. Fix pmc invocation to pass GET PARENT_DATA_SET as a single quoted argument. In both the test helper (ptp-operator-conformance-test) and any exec.Command / shell call in linuxptp-daemon, ensure the action+management-ID are passed as one argument string (e.g. exec.Command("pmc", ..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET")), or switch to the interactive RunPMCExpGetParentDS path with lowercase command ("get PARENT_DATA_SET") to match the RHCOS 10.2 pmc binary's expected input. This resolves the remaining 5 failures.

3. Add a pre-flight GM-lock gate to the CI job. Before running any BC/slave/follower suite, assert that the T-GM node has clock class ≤ 7 (LOCKED); fail fast with a clear infra error rather than letting all 4 suites time out. This would surface the GNSS issue immediately and save ~30 min of wasted CI time per run.


Overall Issue Class: test_flake (dominant: GNSS hardware failure is a known lab environmental flake) + test_regression (secondary: pmc argument bug introduced with RHCOS 10.2 / linuxptp upstream sync 0459bc0)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 25/25 failures, 4/4 suites completely down in a nightly periodic job.

• Root Cause: The openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (registered in linuxptp-daemon/pkg/metrics/register.go:57-63 with labels {process, node, iface}) is only set when UpdateClockStateMetrics() in pkg/metrics/exporter.go:15-23 is called with a servo state change for that interface. A metric label combination {node=cnfdf31, iface=ens2f0np0} being entirely absent means linuxptp-daemon on cnfdf31 never processed a servo state log line for ens2f0np0 — i.e., ptp4l never elected that port as SLAVE and never emitted a servo lock event. The explicit role mismatch (expected=SLAVE, got=LISTENING) in Test 3 for ens2f3np3 corroborates this: both follower ports are stuck in LISTENING/unelected state. The tgm suite failures (clock class 248 = freerun, never 6) indicate the upstream Grandmaster never achieved GNSS lock; with the T-GM in freerun the entire downstream chain — BC → dualfollower slave — cannot lock, which is consistent with all suites failing in the same run. Additionally, the pmc bad command: GET / PARENT_DATA_SET errors in the dualnicbc suite (same run, linuxptp-daemon branch release-5.0) indicate a ptp4l management interface issue. The commit OCPBUGS-90708: Sync from upstream (22-Jun-2026) in linuxptp-daemon (merged 2026-06-23, two days after this run) and the commit OCPBUGS-88369: Fix CLOCK_REALTIME dual-publisher conflict in ParseTBCLogs in cloud-event-proxy (merged 2026-06-25) were both pending at the time of this job.
